articles.title: Ethiopia must not be dragged back into war
articles.summary: TPLF hardliners, backed by Eritrea, are threatening to drag Ethiopia back into a war its people have already rejected.
articles.titleZh: 埃塞俄比亚决不能再次陷入战争
articles.summaryZh: 蒂格雷人阵强硬派在厄立特里亚的支持下，威胁要将埃塞俄比亚拖入一场其人民已经拒绝的战争。

contentParagraphs.en: In October 2022, the negotiating teams from the Ethiopian federal government and the Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) met in Pretoria, South Africa, to make peace.Our meeting and talks took place amidst a raging war that was wreaking havoc on our home country. Given the circumstances, our South African hosts were justified in taking some precautions to keep the two negotiation teams apart and at some distance. It was as if the protocol and security officers assigned by the host country were afraid that the negotiation teams from the two warring parties would get into a fist fight in the middle of the conference hall if they were not shepherded to steer clear of one another. But soon, our hosts were pleasantly surprised to see that being civil to one another was not going to be difficult for anyone in the room.Relieved by the unexpectedly cordial tone set by the delegation heads from both sides, our hosts and mediators allowed the negotiators to engage directly, in a smaller room and in a less formal setting, dispensing with the need to use English as a medium of communication. With the mediators closely following our progress and stepping in to help resolve deadlocks, we held the talks for several days, at times going well beyond midnight. There were heated debates and difficult discussions. Many times, we came close to a breakdown in the talks. But we persisted and, with the support of the elder African statesmen and women who were facilitating the talks, we had more breakthroughs than breakdowns.In this process, although we came from different sides in a deadly internal conflict, we were united in our resolve to make peace. We wanted to see the fighting come to an end. We wanted to put an end to the bloodletting. We were appalled at how the promise of a new generation was being wasted on battlefields across northern Ethiopia. We were also in agreement on the principles that should provide the framework for any agreement we would conclude. From the outset, our discussion focused on the fundamental principles that should inform our negotiation and its outcomes. Once we agreed on a set of principles as norms to underpin the agreement, the remaining task was fleshing out and operationalising these principles. This was not an easy or straightforward undertaking. There was an active war being fought as we were negotiating. Sometimes emotions ran high and the pressure was taking its toll on all of us. But we were determined to seek common ground and make peace.Against the odds, particularly given Ethiopia’s modern history, in which negotiated settlements and compromises are quite rare, we succeeded. An Agreement for Lasting Peace Through a Permanent Cessation of Hostilities between the Government of the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia and the Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) was signed on November 3, 2022.The signing of the agreement was welcomed and celebrated back at home. Ethiopians from all walks of life felt relieved and glad that the war was coming to an end. But not everyone shared these sentiments. In Ethiopia, extremist elements both within the TPLF and the Amhara Fano militia were dismayed by the agreement.The TPLF hardliners wanted to have a short-term ceasefire that would allow them to regroup and continue the war. They did not want a permanent cessation of hostilities. No matter the cost, especially to the young men and women they were using as cannon fodder in the war, they wanted to continue fighting. They were of the view that with a temporary halt in fighting, they would be able to rearm, take the initiative, and get the upper hand.The extremist elements within the Fano also wanted the fighting to continue. In public, their complaint was that the federal government was conceding too much and being too lenient towards the TPLF. But as subsequent events have made abundantly clear, they had other motivations for wanting the fighting to continue. Equally, if not more consequential in its opposition to the Pretoria Agreement, was the Government of Eritrea. Eritrean President Isaias Afwerki even went to the extent of publicly denouncing the agreement as a CIA ploy that did not serve the interests of the region.For President Isayas, the internal conflict in Ethiopia was a dream come true. He wanted both sides to keep on fighting and to see Ethiopia bleed to death. He perceived reconciliation and peace in Ethiopia as a threat. In his quest to see an Ethiopia that is weak, fractured, and susceptible to his machinations, he has long supported various rebel and militant groups against the Ethiopian state. He did not want to see the Pretoria Agreement put an end to the crisis and suffering in Ethiopia that was giving him so much joy and comfort. So, he sought out the extremist elements in the TPLF and continued cultivating similar elements within the Fano. Eritrean operatives brokered an alliance of those disgruntled by the Pretoria Agreement. The forces intent on perpetuating a state of hostility came together under Eritrean tutelage in a band of misfits they dubbed Tsimdo.Right now, this alliance, cobbled together in covert and not so covert meetings in Asmara, Mekelle, and Sudan, is poised to trigger another round of conflict. Aided and abetted by Asmara, the hardline rump of the TPLF has decided to launch an offensive against the federal government in the coming days. In clear violation of the Pretoria Agreement, the rump TPLF has dismantled the regional interim administration and set up its own illegal administration. It has continued recruiting, training, and arming a sizeable fighting force with the direct support of the Eritrean government. It has also purged and cast away all those within its ranks who advocated for peace and adherence to the Pretoria Agreement.The people of the Tigray regional state have categorically rejected the war agenda and the reckless belligerence of the rump TPLF. But taking a few pages from the Eritrean playbook, the TPLF is now e
contentParagraphs.zh: 2022 年 10 月，埃塞俄比亚联邦政府和提格雷人民解放阵线 (TPLF) 的谈判小组在南非比勒陀利亚举行会议，以实现和平。我们的会面和会谈是在我们祖国遭受严重破坏的激烈战争中进行的。鉴于这种情况，我们的南非东道主有理由采取一些预防措施，使两个谈判小组保持一定距离。就好像东道国派来的礼宾和安保人员担心，如果不加以引导，交战双方的谈判小组就会在会议厅中央打起来。但很快，我们的东道主惊喜地发现，对房间里的任何人来说，彼此保持礼貌并不困难。双方代表团团长出人意料的亲切语气让我们的东道主和调解员松了一口气，允许谈判人员在较小的房间和不太正式的环境中直接参与，无需使用英语作为沟通媒介。由于调解员密切关注我们的进展并介入帮助解决僵局，我们举行了几天的会谈，有时甚至持续到午夜之后。大家进行了激烈的辩论和艰难的讨论。很多次，我们的谈判都接近破裂。但我们坚持了下来，在促成谈判的非洲老一辈政治家和妇女的支持下，我们的突破多于失败。在这一过程中，尽管我们来自不同的阵营，陷入致命的内部冲突，但我们团结一致，决心实现和平。我们希望看到战斗结束。我们想结束流血事件。我们对新一代人的希望被浪费在埃塞俄比亚北部的战场上感到震惊。我们还就一些原则达成了一致，这些原则应为我们将达成的任何协议提供框架。从一开始，我们的讨论就集中在应该为我们的谈判及其结果提供依据的基本原则上。一旦我们商定了一套原则作为支撑协议的规范，剩下的任务就是充实和实施这些原则。这不是一件容易或直接的事情。当我们谈判时，正在进行一场激烈的战争。有时情绪高涨，压力让我们所有人都付出了代价。但我们决心寻求共同点并实现和平。尽管困难重重，特别是考虑到埃塞俄比亚的现代历史，通过谈判解决和妥协的情况相当罕见，但我们成功了。 2022年11月3日，埃塞俄比亚联邦民主共和国政府与提格雷人民解放阵线（TPLF）签署了《通过永久停止敌对行动实现持久和平的协议》。该协议的签署在国内受到欢迎和庆祝。埃塞俄比亚各界人士对战争即将结束感到欣慰和高兴。但并非所有人都认同这些观点。在埃塞俄比亚，TPLF 和阿姆哈拉·法诺民兵内部的极端分子都对该协议感到沮丧。TPLF 强硬派希望实现短期停火，以便他们能够重新集结并继续战争。他们不希望永久停止敌对行动。无论付出什么代价，尤其是对于他们在战争中充当炮灰的年轻男女来说，他们都想继续战斗。他们认为，暂时停止战斗，就能重新武装，占据主动，占据上风。法诺内部的极端分子也希望战斗继续下去。在公开场合，他们抱怨联邦政府对TPLF的让步太多、太宽容。但随后的事件已经充分表明，他们希望战斗继续下去还有其他动机。厄立特里亚政府同样反对《比勒陀利亚协定》，甚至影响更大。厄立特里亚总统伊萨亚斯·阿费沃基甚至公开谴责该协议是中央情报局的阴谋，不符合该地区的利益。对于伊萨亚斯总统来说，埃塞俄比亚的内部冲突是梦想成真。他希望双方继续战斗，并看到埃塞俄比亚流血致死。他认为埃塞俄比亚的和解与和平是一种威胁。为了看到一个虚弱、分裂、易受其阴谋影响的埃塞俄比亚，他长期以来一直支持各种反对埃塞俄比亚国家的叛乱和武装团体。他不希望看到《比勒陀利亚协议》结束埃塞俄比亚的危机和苦难，而埃塞俄比亚的危机和苦难曾给他带来如此多的欢乐和安慰。于是，他在TPLF中寻找极端主义分子，并继续在法诺内部培育类似分子。厄立特里亚特工促成了对《比勒陀利亚协定》不满的人结成联盟。意图维持敌对状态的势力在厄立特里亚的监管下聚集在一起，组成了一群不合群的人，他们称之为“齐姆多”。现在，这个在阿斯马拉、默克莱和苏丹举行的秘密或不那么秘密的会议上拼凑起来的联盟，准备引发另一轮冲突。在阿斯马拉的帮助和怂恿下，TPLF 的强硬派决定在未来几天对联邦政府发动攻势。蒂格雷人民阵线解散了地区临时政府并建立了自己的非法政府，这明显违反了《比勒陀利亚协定》。在厄立特里亚政府的直接支持下，它继续招募、训练和武装一支规模庞大的战斗部队。它还清除并抛弃了其队伍中所有主张和平和遵守《比勒陀利亚协定》的人。提格雷地区国家的人民断然拒绝了提格雷地区人民解放阵线的战争议程和鲁莽的好战行为。但从厄立特里亚的剧本来看，TPLF 现在已经

articles.title: AI absolutism is breaking our brains. The apocalyptic future we’re being sold isn’t inevitable
articles.summary:
Nor is the dreamy promise that this tech will unlock boundless potential and productivity
Everything we hear about artificial intelligence is conflicting, and hearing about it feels inescapable. AI is terrible. AI is wonderful. It will break the world. It will transform the futur
articles.titleZh: 人工智能绝对主义正在破坏我们的大脑。我们所面临的世界末日的未来并非不可避免
articles.summaryZh:
这项技术将释放无限潜力和生产力的梦想也不是梦想
我们听到的关于人工智能的一切都是相互矛盾的，而且听到它感觉是不可避免的。人工智能太可怕了。人工智能很棒。它将打破世界。它将改变未来

contentParagraphs.en: Everything we hear about artificial intelligence is conflicting, and hearing about it feels inescapable. AI is terrible. AI is wonderful. It will break the world. It will transform the future. It’s essential to embrace it. It’s a moral imperative to abstain from using it.Already, AI is projected to generate nearly unfathomable amounts of revenue. In the last quarter of 2025, it represented nearly 60% of the growth in the US economy. Already, pundits and economists wring their hands about what calamity will befall us if and when the AI bubble bursts.Since ChatGPT, the first of the large language models, was released in late 2022, more than half a million workers in the tech industry alone have lost their jobs. Any mention of AI tends to be accompanied by warnings that deeper jobs cuts across many more industries are coming for us all. Jensen Huang, CEO of chip giant Nvidia, said in 2025: “Every job will be affected, and immediately. It is unquestionable. You’re not going to lose your job to an AI, but you’re going to lose your job to someone who uses AI.” In January, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei predicted: “AI isn’t a substitute for specific human jobs but rather a general labor substitute for humans.”Increasingly, people young and old flock to a new gold rush in Silicon Valley to toil away on AI-fueled startups. Many of them are driven less by idealistic enthusiasm and more by the dread of missing a ticket for the last train to wealth – and getting stuck forever in the “permanent underclass” that, with any luck, they themselves will create.What all these divergently apocalyptic ideas hold in common is their AI absolutism – a way of seeing AI as a godlike force that will either hasten a golden age of productivity and innovation, or will doom humanity. It mirrors the political polarization of our era and even the zealotry found in religious fanaticism. This is by design. Contradictory as they may be, all these arguments and anxieties fit neatly into the overarching message of the people building this technology: AI’s dominance is inevitable. Get on board or you will be left behind. The robber barons of our age stand to profit wildly from not only enthusiasm about their star product, but also, the terror of it.“If you want to justify this enormous valuation in your IPO, you need to point to the revenue stream that you’re going to generate in the future,” said Suresh Naidu, a professor at Columbia University’s department of economics. “You just need to make it look like you have something that can eat all the work on the planet, so that an investor will think: ‘Oh wow, I don’t want to miss out on this thing.’”Naidu isn’t refuting claims that AI will cut into jobs or upend certain industries. He called the technology “transformative” and said that he uses it every day in his work as a researcher and academic. It’s just that when he zooms out and puts AI and all its attendant promises and warnings in historical context, he sees a lot of hype.There is no control groupAnil Dash, the former CEO of the startup Glitch, who’s been writing about tech for decades, is also unconvinced that the AI we’re being sold will do all the things tech CEOs are predicting it will do.“Any technology that you invest like a trillion dollars into is going to be able to do a lot of things, good or bad. [AI is] a leap forward. I don’t think we’ve ever had a machine learning system that can do as many things as this one does,” he said. But “there’s so much noise that it’s hard to tell what the domains of applicability are.” Coding is an exception, he said. It’s easier to test an AI model’s coding output because it will clearly work, or it won’t. Many other applications for the tech are much more subjective and therefore less prone to immediate job replacement.That’s why the tech industry has made the deepest job cuts so far – though, amid layoffs at companies such as Amazon, Meta and Block, reports from employees have emerged saying the AI productivity gains their bosses trumpet are overblown.Even the role AI is playing in those layoffs and reductions to entry-level positions isn’t entirely clear. Martin Beraja, a professor at UC Berkeley Haas School of Business who studies technological innovation and business cycles, said recent studies that have drawn connections between the release of ChatGPT and a decline in entry-level software jobs are “problematic”.There was “a buildup of jobs in [tech] coming out of the pandemic, and once … consumption patterns moved away from online to the real world again, now we had too many people working in the industry that we didn’t really need”, Beraja said.Some of the biggest and most loudly pro-AI players in tech have arrived at similar conclusions as AI critics. Venture capitalist Marc Andreessen proclaimed in March that overstaffed companies are using AI as a “silver-bullet excuse” to clean house. In May, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman retreated on some of his prior claims of massive job replacement by AI, saying: “I thought there would have been more impact on entry-level white-collar jobs being eliminated by now than ​has actually happened.”And if AI’s worst-case scenario for tech jobs plays out – which would indeed be very bad for many people – that’s still nowhere near the apocalyptic future of labor that many fear. “Is it, in fact, going to destroy all of the jobs?” Naidu asked. “I’m not convinced. Even take software. Software is only about 4 to 6% of GDP. So it’s a lot, but it’s not like the whole economy can be replaced by Claude Code.”Convincing people that AI will replace human workers in droves is a clever marketing tactic. Not only does it stoke rabid investor speculation, but it distracts from a more realistic application of AI for the global workforce, stretching far beyond the borders of the tech industry: using AI to surveil and micromanage employees to squeeze yet more productivity out of them, all the while pressuring them to feel grateful that they have any kind of work at all
contentParagraphs.zh: 我们听到的关于人工智能的一切都是相互矛盾的，而且听到它感觉是不可避免的。人工智能太可怕了。人工智能很棒。它将打破世界。它将改变未来。拥抱它是很重要的。避免使用它是道义上的必要。人工智能预计将产生近乎难以估量的收入。 2025年最后一个季度，它占美国经济增长的近60%。专家和经济学家已经开始担心，如果人工智能泡沫破灭，我们将会遭遇什么灾难。自第一个大型语言模型 ChatGPT 于 2022 年底发布以来，仅科技行业就有超过 50 万工人失业。任何一提到人工智能，往往都会伴随着这样的警告：更多行业的更大规模裁员即将降临到我们所有人身上。芯片巨头英伟达首席执行官黄仁勋在 2025 年表示：“每项工作都会立即受到影响。这是毫无疑问的。你不会因为人工智能而失去工作，但你会因为使用人工智能的人而失去工作。”一月份，Anthropic 首席执行官达里奥·阿莫迪 (Dario Amodei) 预测：“人工智能不会替代特定的人类工作，而是替代人类的一般劳动力。”越来越多的年轻人和老年人涌入硅谷新的淘金热，为人工智能驱动的初创公司辛苦劳作。他们中的许多人更多的是害怕错过通往财富的末班车，并永远陷入“永久下层阶级”的境地，如果幸运的话，他们自己也会创造出这种“永久下层阶级”。所有这些不同的世界末日想法的共同点是他们的人工智能绝对主义——一种将人工智能视为一种神圣力量的方式，它要么会加速生产力和创新的黄金时代，要么会毁灭人类。它反映了我们这个时代的政治两极分化，甚至是宗教狂热中的狂热。这是设计使然。尽管可能相互矛盾，但所有这些争论和焦虑都完美地契合了构建这项技术的人们的总体信息：人工智能的主导地位是不可避免的。上船，否则你就会被抛在后面。我们这个时代的强盗大亨们不仅能从对其明星产品的热情中获得巨额利润，还能从对它的恐惧中获得巨额利润。哥伦比亚大学经济系教授苏雷什·奈杜（Suresh Naidu）表示：“如果你想在首次公开募股中证明如此巨大的估值是合理的，你需要指出你未来将产生的收入来源。” “你只需要让它看起来像是可以吃掉地球上所有工作的东西，这样投资者就会想：‘哇哦，我不想错过这个东西。’”奈杜并没有反驳人工智能将削减就业机会或颠覆某些行业的说法。他称这项技术具有“变革性”，并表示作为研究员和学者，他每天都在工作中使用它。只是当他把目光放远，把人工智能及其所有随之而来的承诺和警告放在历史背景下时，他看到了很多炒作。没有对照组。初创公司 Glitch 的前首席执行官阿尼尔·达什 (Anil Dash) 几十年来一直在撰写有关科技的文章，他也不相信我们正在出售的人工智能能够完成科技首席执行官预测它会做的所有事情。“任何你投资一万亿美元的技术都将能够做很多事情无论好坏，我认为我们从来没有一个机器学习系统可以做这么多事情，”他说。但“噪音太多，很难判断其适用范围。”他说，编码是一个例外。测试人工智能模型的编码输出更容易，因为它显然会起作用，也可能不会。该技术的许多其他应用都更加主观，因此不太容易立即取代工作。这就是为什么科技行业迄今为止进行了最大幅度的裁员——不过，在亚马逊、Meta 和 Block 等公司裁员的过程中，员工的报告称，他们老板吹嘘的人工智能生产力提高有些言过其实。甚至人工智能在这些裁员和初级职位削减中所扮演的角色也不完全清楚。加州大学伯克利分校哈斯商学院研究技术创新和商业周期的教授马丁·贝拉贾 (Martin Beraja) 表示，最近的研究将 ChatGPT 的发布与入门级软件工作岗位的减少联系起来，这是“有问题的”。贝拉贾说，“疫情过后，[科技]领域的工作岗位不断增加，一旦……消费模式再次从网上转移到现实世界，现在我们有太多的人在我们并不真正需要的行业工作。”科技界大力支持人工智能的人士也得出了与人工智能批评者类似的结论。风险投资家马克·安德森 (Marc Andreessen) 在 3 月份宣称，人手过多的公司正在利用人工智能作为清理内部事务的“灵丹妙药”。今年 5 月，OpenAI 首席执行官萨姆·奥尔特曼 (Sam Altman) 对之前关于人工智能大规模取代工作的一些说法进行了反驳，他表示：“我认为，现在入门级白领工作岗位被淘汰所产生的影响会比实际发生的情况更大。”如果人工智能对科技工作的最坏情况发生——这对许多人来说确实非常糟糕——那仍然与许多人担心的劳动力世界末日的未来相去甚远。 “事实上，这会摧毁所有的工作岗位吗？”奈杜问道。 “我不相信。即使以软件为例。软件只占 GDP 的 4% 到 6% 左右。所以数量很多，但并不是整个经济都能被 Claude Code 取代。” 让人们相信人工智能将大量取代人类工人是一种聪明的营销策略。它不仅引发了投资者的狂热猜测，而且分散了人们对人工智能在全球劳动力中更现实的应用的注意力，远远超出了科技行业的边界：使用人工智能来监视和微观管理员工，以榨取更多的生产力，同时迫使他们对自己拥有的任何工作感到感激

articles.title: Ryanair investigated over charging parents to sit with children
articles.summary: The UK's competition regulator is investigating the airline over charges it imposes on parents to sit next to their child.

articles.title: Britain’s defence ⁠minister Healey quits over defence spending
articles.summary: This is a breaking news story.
articles.titleZh: 英国国防部长希利因国防开支辞职
articles.summaryZh: 这是一个突发新闻故事。

contentParagraphs.en: DEVELOPING STORY, PM Starmer is ‘unable to ⁠commit resources ⁠the nation needs’, John Healey ⁠says in letter.⁠Britain’s defence ⁠minister ⁠says he has resigned over ‌a disagreement with the prime minister about defence spending.In a letter addressed to Keir Starmer and posted on X on Thursday, Defence Secretary John Healey accused the prime minister of failing to commit the government resources that ⁠are needed to defend the country.Britain’s’s defence and finance ministries have been locked in talks for months over ‌how to meet rising demands to expand military spending, delaying Britain’s Defence Investment Plan since last year.“You have been unable, and the Treasury has been unwilling, to ⁠commit the resources that ⁠the nation needs to defend the country at this time of rising threats,” Healey ⁠said in his letter to Starmer.The delay has ⁠infuriated Britain’s defence ⁠industry which says it cannot invest in long-term programmes for the country’s security at a time ‌of huge geopolitical volatility and as the United States pivots away ‌from ‌protecting Europe.This is a breaking story. More to come…
contentParagraphs.zh: 故事发展，约翰·希利在信中表示，斯塔默总理“无法投入国家所需的资源”。英国国防部长称，他因与首相在国防开支问题上存在分歧而辞职。周四，国防部长约翰·希利在 X 上发表了一封写给基尔·斯塔默的信，指责首相未能投入保卫国家所需的政府资源。英国国防部和财政部几个月来一直就如何满足不断增长的扩大军费开支的需求进行谈判，自去年以来推迟了英国的国防投资计划。希利在给斯塔默的信中表示，“在威胁日益增加的时刻，你们一直无法、财政部也不愿意投入国家保卫国家所需的资源。”这种拖延激怒了英国的国防工业该公司表示，在地缘政治发生巨大波动且美国不再保护欧洲之际，它无法投资于国家安全的长期计划。这是一个重大新闻。更多内容即将推出……

articles.title: SpaceX is poised for blastoff with an IPO likely to break records
articles.summary: Elon Musk's rocket company, recently merged with xAI, is aiming to raise $75 billion in its initial public stock offering. It's the first of a trio of mega-IPOs from AI companies expected this year.
articles.titleZh: SpaceX 蓄势待发，IPO 有望打破纪录
articles.summaryZh: 埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的火箭公司最近与 xAI 合并，计划通过首次公开募股筹集 750 亿美元资金。这是人工智能公司预计今年将进行三次大型首次公开募股中的第一次。

contentParagraphs.en: SpaceX's mega-rocket Starship makes a test flight from Starbase, Texas, on May 22.
contentParagraphs.zh: SpaceX 的巨型火箭 Starship 于 5 月 22 日从德克萨斯州 Starbase 进行试飞。

contentParagraphs.en: Elon Musk's mega-conglomerate SpaceX is about to launch an initial public offering of stock that is expected to break records — and soon the world will know just how historic that IPO is going to be.
contentParagraphs.zh: 埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 旗下的大型企业 SpaceX 即将进行首次公开​​募股，预计其股票将打破纪录，很快全世界就会知道这次 IPO 将具有多么历史性的意义。

contentParagraphs.en: In a June filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission, SpaceX said it is aiming to raise $75 billion by selling more than 555 million shares at an expected price of $135 each — but the amount raised could be bigger if the initial share price is set higher.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在 6 月份向美国证券交易委员会提交的文件中，SpaceX 表示，其目标是通过以每股 135 美元的预期价格出售超过 5.55 亿股股票来筹集 750 亿美元资金，但如果初始股价定得更高，筹集的金额可能会更大。

contentParagraphs.en: The listing price is expected to be revealed late Thursday, and the stock is expected to start trading publicly on Friday under the ticker SPCX. It could be the biggest initial public offering ever, eclipsing the current record holder, Saudi Aramco, whose 2019 listing netted $29.4 billion.
contentParagraphs.zh: 上市价格预计将于周四晚间公布，该股预计将于周五开始公开交易，股票代码为 SPCX。这可能是有史以来规模最大的首次公开募股，超过了目前的纪录保持者沙特阿美公司，该公司 2019 年的上市净收入为 294 亿美元。

contentParagraphs.en: SpaceX acquired Musk's AI company, xAI, this year, and that makes it part of a trio of huge artificial intelligence-related IPOs in the works. ChatGPT maker OpenAI and Anthropic, owner of the popular Claude AI models, have also filed paperwork with the Securities and Exchange Commission to begin the process of what are sure to be giant IPOs of their own, likely later this year.
contentParagraphs.zh: SpaceX 今年收购了马斯克的人工智能公司 xAI，这使其成为正在进行的三项大型人工智能相关 IPO 的一部分。 ChatGPT 制造商 OpenAI 和广受欢迎的 Claude AI 模型的所有者 Anthropic 也已向美国证券交易委员会提交了文件，开始他们自己的大型 IPO 进程，可能会在今年晚些时候进行。

contentParagraphs.en: Analysts say these three IPOs could reshape markets and inject new volatility into equities, as already sky-high expectations for AI companies come face-to-face with the reality of how these companies are run.
contentParagraphs.zh: 分析师表示，这三起 IPO 可能会重塑市场，并给股市带来新的波动性，因为对人工智能公司本已寄予厚望的公司将面临这些公司运营方式的现实。

contentParagraphs.en: A test of investor appetite
contentParagraphs.zh: 考验投资者的胃口

contentParagraphs.en: The SpaceX IPO is as much a test of investor appetite for rocketry and artificial intelligence as it is for CEO Musk himself, analysts say. "For a lot of people looking at this deal, whether you want to buy it or not is in part a bet on Elon Musk," said Angelo Bochanis, an associate at Renaissance Capital, which tracks IPOs.
contentParagraphs.zh: 分析师表示，SpaceX 的首次公开募股不仅是对投资者对火箭和人工智能兴趣的考验，也是对首席执行官马斯克本人的考验。 “对于很多关注这笔交易的人来说，是否购买这件事在一定程度上是对埃隆·马斯克的押注，”追踪 IPO 的复兴资本 (Renaissance Capital) 合伙人安吉洛·博查尼斯 (Angelo Bochanis) 表示。

contentParagraphs.en: According to the company's listing prospectus, Musk will retain a tight grip on the company by controlling the board and holding more than 80% of stock voting power.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据该公司的上市招股说明书，马斯克将通过控制董事会并持有超过80%的股票投票权来保持对公司的严格控制。

contentParagraphs.en: That carries potential risk, Bochanis said. Musk is one of the best-known figures in corporate America and has made Tesla the most valuable car company in the world. But Bochanis notes that Tesla's share price — and profits in 2025 — took a hit when Musk turned his focus last year to leading DOGE, President Trump's controversial effort to ostensibly make government more efficient by cutting back federal programs and staffing.
contentParagraphs.zh: 博查尼斯说，这带来了潜在的风险。马斯克是美国企业界最知名的人物之一，他使特斯拉成为世界上最有价值的汽车公司。但博查尼斯指出，当马斯克去年将注意力转向领导 DOGE 时，特斯拉的股价以及 2025 年的利润受到了打击。DOGE 是特朗普总统一项颇具争议的举措，表面上是通过削减联邦项目和人员配置来提高政府效率。

contentParagraphs.en: "There are a lot of people who are really sold on his vision and sold on his expertise running this company thus far," said Bochanis. "There are also a lot of people concerned about the fact that, more than any listing ever, this is a company going public where Elon Musk has near-total say of how everything works. He has his hand in a lot of different jars. His attention is very much divided."
contentParagraphs.zh: 博查尼斯说：“有很多人真正相信他的愿景，也相信他迄今为止经营这家公司的专业知识。” “还有很多人担心这样一个事实：与以往任何上市公司相比，埃隆·马斯克对这家公司的运作方式几乎拥有完全的发言权。他的手涉及很多不同的领域。他的注意力非常分散。”

contentParagraphs.en: IPOs can be a "sobering moment" for big tech companies
contentParagraphs.zh: 对于大型科技公司来说，首次公开募股可能是一个“清醒的时刻”

contentParagraphs.en: The public listing of shares by SpaceX and, later, Anthropic and OpenAI will also open up these companies to scrutiny that, as private firms, they have not yet faced. Listed companies are required to file public quarterly and annual financial reports, as well as other paperwork, giving investors a look under the hood.
contentParagraphs.zh: SpaceX 以及后来的 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 的公开上市也将使这些公司面临作为私营公司尚未面临的审查。上市公司必须提交公开的季度和年度财务报告以及其他文件，让投资者了解幕后情况。

contentParagraphs.en: These IPOs are a "sobering moment," said Songyee Yoon, managing partner at Principal Venture Partners, a fund that focuses on AI. Early investors, like venture funds, buy into the possibilities and promise of startups and new technologies like AI, she said. The public market places more value on profits and practical business models — and at present, the public has limited ability to assess these companies' profitability.
contentParagraphs.zh: 专注于人工智能的基金 Primary Venture Partners 的管理合伙人 Songyee Yoon 表示，这些 IPO 是一个“发人深省的时刻”。她说，风险基金等早期投资者相信初创公司和人工智能等新技术的可能性和前景。公开市场更看重利润和实用的商业模式，而目前公众评估这些公司盈利能力的能力有限。

contentParagraphs.en: "It is true that it's a kind of technology with huge potential, but I think we have to also be grounded in thinking about what we can actually accomplish within reasonable means," she said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “确实，这是一种具有巨大潜力的技术，但我认为我们也必须立足于思考我们在合理的手段下实际上可以实现什么，”她说。

contentParagraphs.en: AI is a relatively new technology, she noted, and its development is constrained by resources, including computing power and energy. Popular cutting-edge AI models, like ChatGPT and Claude, also all tend to represent one form of AI, which may or may not end up being the most scalable and useful, she said. Different AI technologies — as well as other companies — could turn out to be more profitable bets.
contentParagraphs.zh: 她指出，人工智能是一项相对较新的技术，其发展受到计算能力和能源等资源的制约。她说，流行的尖端人工智能模型，如 ChatGPT 和 Claude，也都倾向于代表人工智能的一种形式，这种形式最终可能会或可能不会是最具可扩展性和实用性的。不同的人工智能技术以及其他公司可能会成为更有利可图的赌注。

contentParagraphs.en: "We still are very early in terms of innovation and technology development," she said. "There is a long way to go, which means it will be a very bumpy road."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们在创新和技术开发方面还处于早期阶段，”她说。 “还有很长的路要走，这意味着这将是一条非常坎坷的道路。”

contentParagraphs.en: An infusion of cash — and a volatile market
contentParagraphs.zh: 现金注入——以及动荡的市场

contentParagraphs.en: The SpaceX listing comes at a time when stocks, led by tech companies, have been wobbling after huge run-ups amid mounting concerns about the return on investment for AI. By the closing bell on Wednesday, the Nasdaq composite had shed 7.4% since hitting an all-time high on June 1.
contentParagraphs.zh: SpaceX 上市之际，由于人们对人工智能投资回报的担忧日益加剧，以科技公司为首的股票在大幅上涨后一直在波动。截至周三收盘，纳斯达克综合指数自 6 月 1 日创下历史新高以来已下跌 7.4%。

contentParagraphs.en: The SpaceX IPO — along with those of Anthropic and OpenAI — could also lead investors to shift large chunks of money away from other listed companies and into these new ones. This could spark some market turbulence that could even affect the portfolios of people who didn't actively buy SpaceX stock.
contentParagraphs.zh: SpaceX 以及 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 的 IPO 也可能导致投资者将大量资金从其他上市公司转移到这些新公司。这可能会引发一些市场动荡，甚至可能影响那些没有积极购买 SpaceX 股票的人的投资组合。

contentParagraphs.en: The Nasdaq, FTSE Russell and other stock index firms have agreed to fast-track the inclusion of SpaceX shares in their indexes, which means passive investment funds that track indexes, like retirement and education funds, will have to acquire the shares.
contentParagraphs.zh: 纳斯达克、富时罗素和其他股指公司已同意快速将 SpaceX 股票纳入其指数，这意味着跟踪指数的被动投资基金（如退休基金和教育基金）将必须收购这些股票。

contentParagraphs.en: "There's a giant risk for volatility," said Bochanis. "If investments are going to get more concentrated, if more people are going to be putting a larger percentage of their money into a couple of names, then that risk of one bad earnings call is really going to be felt by people."
contentParagraphs.zh: “波动风险巨大，”博查尼斯说。 “如果投资变得更加集中，如果更多的人将更大比例的资金投入到几个名字中，那么人们真的会感受到一场糟糕的财报电话会议的风险。”

contentParagraphs.en: Friday's listing also comes at a time when the stock market and the U.S. economy have been propelled by private venture funds and the government investing hundreds of billions of dollars into artificial intelligence companies.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周五的上市也是在私募风险基金和政府向人工智能公司投资数千亿美元推动股市和美国经济之际进行的。

contentParagraphs.en: At its targeted share price of $135, SpaceX's valuation would reach around $1.75 trillion, making it one of the 10 biggest listed companies on Earth. Analysts say Anthropic and OpenAI
contentParagraphs.zh: 按照 135 美元的目标股价计算，SpaceX 的估值将达到 1.75 万亿美元左右，使其成为地球上十大上市公司之一。分析师称 Anthropic 和 OpenAI

articles.title: FBI seizing evidence at California plant where chemical tank overheated
articles.summary: Federal authorities served a search warrant on Wednesday at a Southern California aerospace facility where a chemical tank overheated last month, forcing 50,000 residents to evacuate because authorities feared an explosion.
articles.titleZh: FBI 在加州工厂查获化学罐过热的证据
articles.summaryZh: 周三，联邦当局对南加州的一家航空航天设施发出了搜查令，该设施上个月的一个化学罐过热，迫使 5 万名居民撤离，因为当局担心发生爆炸。

contentParagraphs.en: Ariel view of the chemical tank at GKN Aerospace in Garden Grove, Calif., on Wednesday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周三，阿里尔 (Ariel) 查看位于加利福尼亚州加登格罗夫的吉凯恩航空航天公司的化学储罐。

contentParagraphs.en: LOS ANGELES — Federal authorities served a search warrant on Wednesday at a Southern California aerospace facility where a chemical tank overheated last month, forcing 50,000 residents to evacuate because authorities feared a catastrophic explosion.
contentParagraphs.zh: 洛杉矶——周三，联邦当局对南加州一处航空设施发出了搜查令，该设施上个月的一个化学罐过热，迫使 5 万名居民撤离，因为当局担心发生灾难性爆炸。

contentParagraphs.en: The warrant signed by a federal judge last week approved the seizure of documents and records related to the "storage, use, or disposal" of methyl methacrylate, the chemical inside the affected tank.
contentParagraphs.zh: 联邦法官上周签署的搜查令批准扣押与甲基丙烯酸甲酯（受影响储罐内的化学物质）“储存、使用或处置”相关的文件和记录。

contentParagraphs.en: "Samples of the substance within any tank, tote, drum, vat, vessel, or container suspected of containing or having previously contained methyl methacrylate and/or any hazardous substance" were also sought, according to the warrant.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据搜查令，还要求“在任何疑似含有或曾经含有甲基丙烯酸甲酯和/或任何危险物质的罐、手提包、桶、大桶、容器或容器内寻找物质样本”。

contentParagraphs.en: The warrant also orders agents to seize records related to "any cooling equipment or other equipment used to control or regulate the temperature of methyl methacrylate."
contentParagraphs.zh: 该搜查令还命令特工扣押与“任何用于控制或调节甲基丙烯酸甲酯温度的冷却设备或其他设备”相关的记录。

contentParagraphs.en: The FBI confirmed its agents were searching GKN Aerospace Transparency Systems in the Orange County city of Garden Grove. Multiple vehicles and several federal agents were seen outside the facility Wednesday morning.
contentParagraphs.zh: 联邦调查局证实，其特工正在奥兰治县加登格罗夫市搜查吉凯恩航空透明系统。周三上午，在该设施外看到多辆车辆和几名联邦特工。

contentParagraphs.en: Company says it's cooperating with authorities
contentParagraphs.zh: 公司表示正在与当局合作

contentParagraphs.en: GKN Aerospace makes cockpit windows, canopies and windshields. The tank that overheated contained 6,000 to 7,000 gallons (22,700 to 26,500 liters) of methyl methacrylate, which is highly flammable. The liquid is used in the manufacturing of plastics and coatings, such as Plexiglas and dental prosthetics.
contentParagraphs.zh: 吉凯恩航空航天公司生产驾驶舱窗户、座舱盖和挡风玻璃。过热的储罐内装有 6,000 至 7,000 加仑（22,700 至 26,500 升）的甲基丙烯酸甲酯，该物质高度易燃。该液体用于制造塑料和涂料，例如有机玻璃和假牙。

contentParagraphs.en: Exposure to the chemical can cause serious respiratory problems, neurological issues and irritation to the skin, eyes and throat, according to the Environmental Protection Agency.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据环境保护局称，接触这种化学物质可能会导致严重的呼吸系统问题、神经系统问题以及皮肤、眼睛和喉咙的刺激。

contentParagraphs.en: The chemical that overheated is still in the holding tank, according to the Orange County Health Care Agency, which is leading the site cleanup and waste removal efforts.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据奥兰治县卫生保健机构称，过热的化学品仍在储罐中，该机构正在领导现场清理和废物清除工作。

contentParagraphs.en: The agency had planned to pump the neutralized methyl methacrylate from the storage tanks into sealed trucks for transport and disposal starting at the end of last week, according to a press release on the agency's website. But on Friday, they said the removal didn't happen "due to unavailable resources."
contentParagraphs.zh: 据该机构网站上的一份新闻稿称，该机构计划从上周末开始将储存罐中中和的甲基丙烯酸甲酯泵入密封卡车进行运输和处置。但周五，他们表示“由于资源不可用”，此次删除并未发生。

contentParagraphs.en: Once a new date is confirmed, they will provide advance notice to the community.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一旦新日期确定，他们将提前通知社区。

contentParagraphs.en: Responding to a request for comment on the FBI investigation, a GKN spokesperson told the AP on Wednesday morning: "We are cooperating with authorities at our Garden Grove facility and will continue to do so."
contentParagraphs.zh: 在回应对联邦调查局调查发表评论的请求时，吉凯恩发言人周三上午告诉美联社：“我们正在与加登格罗夫工厂的当局合作，并将继续这样做。”

contentParagraphs.en: GKN Aerospace's Steve Carlin spoke at a community meeting Tuesday evening. He thanked the firefighters and local leaders who responded to the incident at the plant that employs more than 500 people, and apologized to the community.
contentParagraphs.zh: 吉凯恩航空航天公司的史蒂夫·卡林在周二晚上的社区会议上发表了讲话。他感谢消防员和当地领导人对这家拥有 500 多名员工的工厂的事件做出反应，并向社区道歉。

contentParagraphs.en: FBI agents stage at GKN Aerospace in Garden Grove, Calif., on Wednesday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周三，联邦调查局特工在加利福尼亚州加登格罗夫的吉凯恩航空航天公司出动。

contentParagraphs.en: "On behalf of GKN and the Garden Grove plant I want to say that I'm sorry that this event and this incident occurred. I understand and I realized sitting here tonight what a disruptive event it was and how unsettling it is to the greater community. Particularly unsettling to us at GKN because of the long history that we have with Garden Grove and how connected we are to this community."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我谨代表 GKN 和加登格罗夫工厂对发生的这一事件和事件表示抱歉。我理解并意识到今晚坐在这里这是一个多么具有破坏性的事件，对更大的社区来说是多么令人不安。尤其让我们 GKN 感到不安，因为我们与加登格罗夫有着悠久的历史，以及我们与这个社区的紧密联系。”

contentParagraphs.en: Garden Grove city leaders and residents urged GKN Aerospace to consider moving these tanks of methyl methacrylate off of the Garden Grove plant, so the chemical would be far away from residents and businesses. But Carlin said it is very early in the investigation into what happened, so it is too soon to decide what the company might do in response to the incident. He promised to be transparent with the community about the investigation.
contentParagraphs.zh: 加登格罗夫市领导和居民敦促吉凯恩航空航天公司考虑将这些甲基丙烯酸甲酯罐从加登格罗夫工厂移走，这样化学品就会远离居民和企业。但卡林表示，目前对所发生事件的调查还处于早期阶段，因此现在决定公司可能采取的应对措施还为时过早。他承诺对社区的调查保持透明。

contentParagraphs.en: Overheating tank risked a catastrophic explosion
contentParagraphs.zh: 储罐过热有发生灾难性爆炸的危险

contentParagraphs.en: The incident was reported on May 21 and evacuations began the next day. The tank overheated because a valve on the cooling system that kept it at 50 degrees Fahrenheit (10 degrees Celsius) failed, officials said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该事件于 5 月 21 日报道，第二天开始疏散。官员称，由于冷却系统上保持 50 华氏度（10 摄氏度）温度的阀门出现故障，导致储罐过热。

contentParagraphs.en: Crews sprayed water on the tank until the interior temperature stabilized to 92 F (33.3 C), down from 100 F (37.7 C). A sprinkler system was used to douse the tank, and the company said its technical specialists and firefighters removed insulation from the tank to help cool it.
contentParagraphs.zh: 工作人员向水箱喷水，直到内部温度从 100 华氏度（37.7 摄氏度）稳定到 92 华氏度（33.3 摄氏度）。该公司使用喷水灭火系统来浇灭水箱，该公司表示，其技术专家和消防员拆除了水箱的隔热层以帮助冷却。

contentParagraphs.en: A crack that formed by chance on the tank relieved pressure and helped avert a catastrophic explosion, allowing most evacuees to return home over the Memorial Day weekend. Authorities announced they were lifting the final orders after the temperature on the tank remained stable for four hours without intervention from sprinklers.
contentParagraphs.zh: 储罐上偶然形成的裂缝缓解了压力，并帮助避免了灾难性爆炸，使大多数疏散人员在阵亡将士纪念日周末返回家中。当局宣布，在罐体温度在没有洒水装置干预的情况下保持稳定四个小时后，他们将取消最终命令。

contentParagraphs.en: Separately, the Orange County District Attorney's Office also is conducting a criminal investigation into the GKN Aerospace plant, according to DA spokesperson Kimberly Edds.
contentParagraphs.zh: 另外，据奥兰治县地方检察官办公室发言人金伯利·埃兹 (Kimberly Edds) 称，奥兰治县地方检察官办公室也在对吉凯恩航空航天工厂进行刑事调查。

contentParagraphs.en: "We have sent a preservation letter to GKN directing them not to modify or destroy any evidence, which the company's outside counsel confirmed receipt," Edds told The Associated Press in an email.
contentParagraphs.zh: 埃兹在一封电子邮件中告诉美联社：“我们已向吉凯恩发送了一封保全信，指示他们不要修改或销毁任何证据，该公司的外部法律顾问已确认收到该信。”

contentParagraphs.en: Lawyers in federal lawsuits welcome the FBI's involvement
contentParagraphs.zh: 联邦诉讼律师欢迎联邦调查局介入

contentParagraphs.en: About a dozen people and businesses that were among the 50,000 evacuated during the chemical emergency have filed lawsuits against the company. Some residents reported strong odors, respiratory irritation, headaches and dizziness. They question why the chemical plant was allowed to operate so close to homes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在化学品紧急情况期间疏散的 50,000 人中，约有十几个人和企业已对该公司提起诉讼。一些居民报告有强烈的气味、呼吸道刺激、头痛和头晕。他们质疑为什么允许化工厂在离家如此近的地方运营。

contentParagraphs.en: Lawyer Rickard McCune represents Big Rob's Pizzeria and Fruit Caboose Concessions in a federal lawsuit claiming GKN Aerospace and parent company Melrose Industries were negligent and put the surrounding communities at risk. He said they're pleased the federal government is investigating. The FBI's involvement will help bring justice to those who were harmed, he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 律师 Rickard McCune 在一项联邦诉讼中代表 Big Rob's Pizzeria 和 Fruit Caboose Concessions 提起诉讼，指控吉凯恩航空航天公司和母公司梅尔罗斯工业公司疏忽大意，将周围社区置于危险之中。他说他们很高兴联邦政府正在进行调查。他说，联邦调查局的介入将有助于为那些受到伤害的人伸张正义。

contentParagraphs.en: Another lawyer, Alex Wheeler, represents Dinh Tran and Drippys Gourmet Ice Cream Sandwiches and said they're relieved that the FBI is using its resources to investigate potential criminal acts.
contentParagraphs.zh: 另一位代表 Dinh Tran 和 Drippys Gourmet Ice Cream Sandwiches 的律师 Alex Wheeler 表示，联邦调查局正在利用其资源调查潜在的犯罪行为，他们感到很欣慰。

contentParagraphs.en: "As more information becomes public, it's becoming clearer and cleare
contentParagraphs.zh: “随着更多信息公开，它变得越来越清晰

articles.title: From neon mosquitoes to winged migrations, top images captured by scientists
articles.summary: Five winning images from a photo contest show scientists at work and capture the wonder of research and discovery.
articles.titleZh: 从霓虹灯蚊子到带翅膀的迁徙，科学家捕捉到的顶级图像
articles.summaryZh: 摄影比赛中的五张获奖照片展示了科学家的工作方式，捕捉了研究和发现的奇迹。

contentParagraphs.en: Lee Haines, a vector biologist at the University of Notre Dame peers into a microscope at a mosquito. "It looks like I'm traveling through space, doesn't it?" she asks of the photo, a winning image in the Scientists at Work photo contest.
contentParagraphs.zh: 圣母大学的媒介生物学家李·海恩斯（Lee Haines）在显微镜下观察蚊子。 “看起来我正在穿越太空，不是吗？”她询问了这张照片，这是科学家工作摄影大赛的获奖照片。

contentParagraphs.en: Shayanta Chowdhury
contentParagraphs.zh: 沙彦塔·乔杜里

contentParagraphs.en: Shayanta Chowdhury
contentParagraphs.zh: 沙彦塔·乔杜里

contentParagraphs.en: For new discoveries, everyday mysteries, and the science behind the headlines, follow NPR's Short Wave podcast .
contentParagraphs.zh: 如需了解新发现、日常奥秘以及头条新闻背后的科学，请关注 NPR 的短波播客。

contentParagraphs.en: The mood was tense one fall morning in a village in Spain.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一个秋天的早晨，西班牙一个村庄的气氛很紧张。

contentParagraphs.en: A conservation and research group called Waldrappteam was nearing the end of a remarkable undertaking — escorting a flock of 36 northern bald ibises along their migratory route from southeastern Germany to the highlands of southern Spain.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一个名为 Waldrappteam 的保护和研究小组即将完成一项非凡的任务——护送 36 只北方秃鹮沿着从德国东南部到西班牙南部高地的迁徙路线。

contentParagraphs.en: The northern bald ibis has a ruddy bill and a crest of feathers that look like an intermittent mohawk. It disappeared from Europe some 400 years ago due to overhunting.
contentParagraphs.zh: 北方秃鹮的喙呈红色，羽毛冠状，看起来像断断续续的莫霍克发型。大约400年前，由于过度捕猎，它从欧洲消失了。

contentParagraphs.en: But, says Gunnar Hartmann, an undergraduate majoring in biogeoscience at the University of Koblenz in Germany, another population of the ibises was found living in Syria and Morocco a century ago. Scientists at the time brought some of the birds to Europe to rear their chicks in captivity where they can form bonds with their human handlers. Now scientists teach them to migrate, guiding them along their 1,700-plus-mile route.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但是，德国科布伦茨大学生物地球科学专业的本科生古纳尔·哈特曼 (Gunnar Hartmann) 表示，一个世纪前在叙利亚和摩洛哥发现了另一群朱鹮。当时，科学家们将一些鸟类带到了欧洲，圈养它们的雏鸟，在那里它们可以与人类饲养员建立联系。现在，科学家们教它们如何迁徙，引导它们沿着 1,700 多英里的路线行走。

contentParagraphs.en: In the fall of 2024, Hartmann joined Waldrappteam for 50 days as they flew an ultralight aircraft across southern Germany, France and Spain, showing the latest group of ibises their way.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2024 年秋天，哈特曼加入 Waldrapp 团队，为期 50 天，驾驶超轻型飞机飞越德国南部、法国和西班牙，为最新的朱鹮群引路。

contentParagraphs.en: Ibises take flight, led by scientists in an ultralight aircraft in this image which was the overall winner of this year's Scientist at Work photography competition sponsored by the journal Nature.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这张照片中，朱鹮在科学家的带领下乘坐超轻型飞机起飞，该照片是《自然》杂志主办的今年科学家在职摄影比赛的总冠军。

contentParagraphs.en: Gunnar Hartmann
contentParagraphs.zh: 冈纳·哈特曼

contentParagraphs.en: Gunnar Hartmann
contentParagraphs.zh: 冈纳·哈特曼

contentParagraphs.en: It was on a cool, rosemary-scented morning in Spain, in the town of Jaén in Andalusia, that Hartmann, who was the project's photographer, snapped an image with his camera that would become the overall winner of this year's Scientist at Work photography competition sponsored by the journal Nature. The winners were announced Wednesday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在西班牙安达卢西亚哈恩镇的一个凉爽、迷迭香味的早晨，该项目的摄影师哈特曼用他的相机拍摄了一张照片，这张照片成为今年由《自然》杂志主办的“工作中的科学家”摄影比赛的总冠军。获奖者于周三公布。

contentParagraphs.en: In the photo, the aircraft soars in the sky beneath a yellow parachute. Nineteen of the birds flap ahead of it, even though they're the ones following the people. A golden landscape sprawls below.
contentParagraphs.zh: 照片中，飞机在黄色降落伞下翱翔在天空。十九只鸟在它前面拍打翅膀，尽管它们是跟着人们的。下面是一片金色的风景。

contentParagraphs.en: Hartmann is pleased to have brought attention to the conservation efforts bringing the northern bald ibis back to Europe.
contentParagraphs.zh: 哈特曼很高兴引起人们对将北方秃鹮带回欧洲的保护工作的关注。

contentParagraphs.en: "For me, this special morning was super emotional," recalls Hartmann. They'd already been flying for days and the ibises were tired. "We were struggling to motivate the birds to follow the aircraft to get them to do what we wanted them to do — what they need to do to be a good migratory bird."
contentParagraphs.zh: “对我来说，这个特别的早晨非常激动，”哈特曼回忆道。他们已经飞行了好几天，朱鹮已经累了。 “我们一直在努力激励鸟类跟随飞机，让它们做我们希望它们做的事情——它们需要做的事情才能成为一只优秀的候鸟。”

contentParagraphs.en: But eventually the ibises launched themselves into the air and followed the ultralight. Hartmann positioned himself on a nearby hill, hoping for the perfect shot.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但最终朱鹮飞向空中并跟随超轻型飞机。哈特曼站在附近的一座山上，希望能拍到完美的照片。

contentParagraphs.en: "I was part of the project — I was just living it," he says. "And then I came out of this bubble and then I realized how it must feel to see a picture of it. There is something romantic in it."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我是这个项目的一部分——我只是享受其中，”他说。 “然后我走出了这个泡沫，然后我意识到看到它的照片一定是什么感觉。里面有一些浪漫的东西。”

contentParagraphs.en: A watery world in technicolor
contentParagraphs.zh: 色彩缤纷的水世界

contentParagraphs.en: Allen Tian, a PhD student at Queen's University in Ontario, Canada, is another one of the winners. He captured an arresting overhead image of an algal bloom in Dog Lake in Ontario — a massive growth of phytoplankton that usually occurs due to excess nutrients in the water.
contentParagraphs.zh: 加拿大安大略省女王大学的博士生艾伦·田（Allen Tian）是另一位获奖者。他捕捉到了安大略省狗湖中藻类大量繁殖的引人注目的俯视图像——浮游植物的大量生长，通常是由于水中营养过剩而发生的。

contentParagraphs.en: A team of scientists studies an algae bloom in a lake in Ontario. They are researching how to monitor and predict the blooms, which cause environmental and economic harm.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一组科学家研究安大略湖中的藻华。他们正在研究如何监测和预测会造成环境和经济危害的水华。

contentParagraphs.en: "Even though they were really putrid from the ground and they smelled awful and they looked like pea soup and they were well known for poisoning local dogs and livestock," he says, "they were also really beautiful from the air."
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说：“尽管它们从地面上看确实已经腐烂，闻起来很糟糕，看起来像豌豆汤，而且以毒害当地的狗和牲畜而闻名，但从空中看它们也非常美丽。”

contentParagraphs.en: The photograph is an otherworldly undulating green. "I like to think of it as impressionist art," he says. "The paint-like consistency of the algal bloom — it gets pulled in different directions by wind, by currents, but also by the movements of living things."
contentParagraphs.zh: 照片是超凡脱俗的起伏绿色。 “我喜欢将其视为印象派艺术，”他说。 “藻华像油漆一样的稠度——它被风、水流以及生物的运动拉向不同的方向。”

contentParagraphs.en: A single miniscule boat etches a path on the water's surface. Tian's research assistant is standing up and her shadow falls upon the chartreuse murk. The team studies how to monitor and predict the blooms, which cause environmental and economic harm.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一艘微型船在水面上刻出一条路径。田的研究助理站了起来，她的影子落在黄绿色的暗色上。该团队研究如何监测和预测会造成环境和经济危害的水华。

contentParagraphs.en: Another winning photograph shows a marine biologist off of western Australia sampling the microbes living on the skin of a wild whale shark. The image elicits awe in Rob Harcourt who took the photograph. He's also an emeritus professor of marine ecology at Macquarie University in Sydney, Australia.
contentParagraphs.zh: 另一张获奖照片显示，一位来自澳大利亚西部的海洋生物学家正在对野生鲸鲨皮肤上的微生物进行采样。这张照片引起了拍摄这张照片的罗布·哈考特 (Rob Harcourt) 的敬畏。他还是澳大利亚悉尼麦考瑞大学海洋生态学名誉教授。

contentParagraphs.en: Marine biologist Michael Doane collects samples of microorganisms living on the skin of a whale shark.
contentParagraphs.zh: 海洋生物学家迈克尔·多恩收集了鲸鲨皮肤上的微生物样本。

contentParagraphs.en: "We leap into the blue when we find a marine giant," he says. "We collect samples through immense effort that are revealing so much both about these elusive sharks and the environment they inhabit and how it is changing with human stressors such as climate change."
contentParagraphs.zh: “当我们发现海洋巨人时，我们就会跳入蓝色，”他说。 “我们通过巨大的努力收集了样本，这些样本揭示了这些难以捉摸的鲨鱼和它们栖息的环境，以及它如何随着气候变化等人类压力而变化。”

contentParagraphs.en: Uli Kunz, a freelance marine biologist and photographer, reveals a different aspect of underwater life in his winning image. Two scientists gaze upon a coral specimen on the sandy floor of the Red Sea off the coast of Saudi Arabia. It's inside a transparent incubation chamber where its metabolism can be measured.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自由海洋生物学家兼摄影师乌利·昆兹 (Uli Kunz) 在其获奖照片中揭示了水下生物的不同侧面。两名科学家正在观察沙特阿拉伯海岸附近红海沙床上的珊瑚标本。它位于透明的孵化室内，可以测量其新陈代谢。

contentParagraphs.en: The shot took work. "I placed a diving torch behind the chamber," says Kunz. "Then I had to position the researchers' heads with millimeter precision, constantly checking the image on my camera to capture the reflections in their masks and this moment of shared contemplation."
contentParagraphs.zh: 拍摄花了功夫。 “我在房间后面放了一个潜水手电筒，”昆兹说。 “然后我必须以毫米精度定位研究人员的头部，不断检查相机上的图像，以捕捉他们面具中的反射和共同沉思的时刻。”

contentParagraphs.en: Researchers study coral in the Red sea off the coast of Saudia Arabia.
contentParagraphs.zh: 研究人员研究沙特阿拉伯海岸附近红海的珊瑚。

contentParagraphs.en: The tiniest of galaxies
contentParagraphs.zh: 最小的星系

contentParagraphs.en: The final winning photograph seemingly fuses the real with the imagined.
contentParagraphs.zh: 最终获奖照片似乎将真实与想象融为一体。

contentParagraphs.en: "It looks like I'm traveling through space, doesn't it?" asks Lee Haines, a vector biolog
contentParagraphs.zh: “看起来我正在穿越太空，不是吗？”媒介生物学家李·海恩斯 (Lee Haines) 问道

articles.title: Social media on trial: Four important cases to watch
articles.summary: Social media firms face thousands of lawsuits, the BBC looks at four which could be significant.
articles.titleZh: 审判中的社交媒体：四个值得关注的重要案件
articles.summaryZh: 社交媒体公司面临数千起诉讼，英国广播公司 (BBC) 调查了四起可能影响重大的诉讼。

contentParagraphs.en: Site search7 hours agoKali HaysTechnology reporter, San FranciscoGetty ImagesSocial media is under intense legal scrutinyWhen social media started to take over the internet 20 years ago, it was widely hailed as a game-changing technology that would connect people across divides and make information more accessible.Today, companies like Meta, owner of Facebook and Instagram, Google, owner of Youtube, and Snapchat, along with relatively newer platforms like TikTok, Discord and social gaming platform Roblox, are facing thousands of lawsuits in the US over claims that they have instead harmed users, children in particular.Taken together, the outcome of the lawsuits, whether they ultimately settle out of court or end up with jury verdicts against companies, could change the way social platforms operate forever."It's created a stage that not only legal observers are watching, but regulators and lawmakers are watching closely as well," Eric Talley, a lawyer and professor at Columbia Law School, said.Talley noted that the way this growing wave of lawsuits against platforms is feeding into broader public perception is likely to influence political elections for the next several years, impacting new and revised laws and regulations.Many of the cases are going through courts in California, where all of the major social platforms are headquartered. Known as the "California effect", legal and policy changes enacted in the state tend to lead to nationwide changes."There's no denying anymore that there is an issue with child safety on the platforms," Alexis Shore Ingber, a communications law expert and a professor at Syracuse University, said. "We are seeing an inflection point. These cases are significant."Already this year, Meta and YouTube notched an unprecedented loss in a case brought by a young woman who claimed she was addicted as a child to social media, contributing to her mental and emotional health struggles. The companies were ordered by a jury to pay her a combined $6m (£4.5m) in damages. Both firms said they disagreed with the verdict and intended to appeal.Meta also lost a bigger case in New Mexico, brought by that state's attorney general accusing the company of essentially misleading the public that its platforms were safe for children despite known issues with young people being sexually exploited on them. Meta said it also plans to appeal against this verdict.During the years these cases were brought and resolved, Meta has released changes to its platforms aimed at making them safer for young users.But broader change to the platforms, how they are designed and function and even accessed, is likely to take years more, and more court rulings against them.Between this year and next, Meta and the other major social platforms are poised to fight their way through more trials where juries could consider a host of claims by young users, their parents, school districts, and state attorneys who allege an array of ill effects from the way social media platforms are designed and operate.Even a billionaire is prepared to take Meta to trial over its hosting of advertisements that scam people out of money.So which cases really matter?The BBC looked through scores of cases in the US to find the handful of lawsuits against social media and social gaming companies that are on track for trial in the next year or so and could have a significant impact on the platforms' businesses and operations.According to Adam J. Schwartz, a lawyer who also founded an online document review tool, the following lawsuits "are the bellwether cases that will set the tone and tenor for shaping the law in the future".Social Media Adolescent Addiction MDLGetty ImagesOne case against social media firms involves more than 1,000 Californian schoolsBroadly, the schools accuse Instagram, YouTube, Snapchat and TikTok of being intentionally designed to be addictive, which has allegedly harmed children mentally and emotionally through their excessive use of platforms. The schools claim that dealing with the ill effects of social media has cost them money and resources, and that the platforms should be deemed a "public nuisance" and held liable for impacting children's well-being.Although a jury trial for certain of the school districts' claims is now set to begin in February, as the platforms recently settled with a school district that was to be the first trial, all of the cases could take a couple more years to resolve completely. Should court outcomes go against the platforms, everything from the way platforms display user engagement to who they allow on the platforms could change.A spokesman for YouTube said: "The allegations in these complaints are simply not true."A spokeswoman for Snapchat said: "We fundamentally disagree with the allegations - we do not target schools."Meta declined to comment and TikTok did not respond to a request for comment.People of the State of California v. MetaAttorneys for California and Colorado led a group of 29 states in filing in 2023 a lawsuit against Meta and Instagram. It is set to go to trial in August.While it is also before the same judge as the MDL in California, the states are accusing Meta alone of violations of the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act, a federal law known as COPPA. The law was intended to protect children under 13 years old from being targeted by businesses operating online but was enacted in 2000.Meta has already provided more than 2 million documents in the case, according to court records.Should the states prevail in their claims, it is demanding that Meta better prevent users under 13 years old from using its platforms and remove data it has previously collected from underage users, along with a host of other changes.Meta uses such data to do things like ad targeting and train its artificial intelligence (AI) models and tools.A spokesman for the company declined to comment.John Doe, a minor v. Roblox et alAFP via Getty ImagesRoblox and Discord face a lawsuit o
contentParagraphs.zh: 现场搜索 7 小时前，旧金山科技记者 Kali Hays 盖蒂图片社社交媒体正受到严格的法律审查当社交媒体 20 年前开始接管互联网时，它被广泛誉为一种改变游戏规则的技术，可以跨越鸿沟将人们联系起来，让信息更容易获取。如今，Facebook 和 Instagram 的所有者 Meta、Youtube 和 Snapchat 的谷歌等公司，以及 TikTok、Discord 和社交游戏平台 Roblox 等相对较新的平台，都面临着法律审查。美国有数千起诉讼，指控它们反而伤害了用户，特别是儿童。总而言之，诉讼的结果，无论最终是庭外和解还是陪审团对公司做出裁决，都可能永远改变社交平台的运作方式。哥伦比亚法学院律师兼教授埃里克·塔利表示：“这创造了一个不仅法律观察员在关注，监管机构和立法者也在密切关注的舞台。”塔利指出，这股不断增长的诉讼浪潮的方式反对平台的行为正在引起更广泛的公众认知，可能会影响未来几年的政治选举，从而影响新的和修订的法律法规。许多案件正在加利福尼亚州法院审理，所有主要社交平台的总部都设在加利福尼亚州。该州颁布的法律和政策变化往往会导致全国范围内的变化，这被称为“加州效应”。“无可否认，平台上存在儿童安全问题，”通信法专家、雪城大学教授亚历克西斯·肖尔·英格贝尔(Alexis Shore Ingber)表示。 “我们正在看到一个拐点。这些案件意义重大。” 今年，Meta 和 YouTube 在一名年轻女性提起的案件中遭受了前所未有的损失，该女性声称自己小时候沉迷于社交媒体，导致她的精神和情感健康陷入困境。陪审团命令两家公司向她支付总计 600 万美元（450 万英镑）的损害赔偿金。两家公司均表示不同意判决，并打算上诉。Meta还在新墨西哥州败诉了一起更大的案件，该州总检察长指控该公司本质上误导公众，认为其平台对儿童是安全的，尽管已知存在年轻人遭受性剥削的问题。 Meta 表示，它还计划对这一判决提出上诉。在这些案件提起和解决的这些年里，Meta 发布了对其平台的更改，旨在让年轻用户更安全。但对平台进行更广泛的更改，包括平台的设计、功能甚至访问方式，可能需要数年时间，以及更多针对它们的法院裁决。在今年和明年之间，Meta 和其他主要社交平台准备通过更多审判，陪审团可以考虑年轻用户、他们的父母、学区和学校的大量索赔。州检察官指控社交媒体平台的设计和运营方式造成了一系列不良影响。即使是亿万富翁也准备将 Meta 因其托管广告骗取人们钱财而告上法庭。那么哪些案件真正重要呢？BBC 查阅了美国的数十起案件，发现了少数针对社交媒体和社交游戏公司的诉讼，这些诉讼预计在明年左右进行审判，并可能对平台的业务和运营产生重大影响。据律师 Adam J. Schwartz 称，还创立了一个在线文件审查工具，以下诉讼“是为未来制定法律定下基调和基调的风向标案件”。 社交媒体青少年成瘾 MDLGetty Images 针对社交媒体公司的一起案件涉及 1000 多所加州学校。从广义上讲，这些学校指控 Instagram、YouTube、Snapchat 和 TikTok 被故意设计成让人上瘾，据称这些平台通过过度使用平台而伤害了儿童的精神和情感。这些学校声称，处理社交媒体的不良影响已经耗费了他们的金钱和资源，这些平台应被视为“公害”，并因影响儿童福祉而承担责任。尽管针对某些学区索赔的陪审团审判现已定于二月开始，但由于这些平台最近与一审的学区达成和解，所有案件可能还需要几年时间才能彻底解决。如果法庭结果对这些平台不利，那么从平台显示用户参与度的方式到允许哪些人进入平台等一切都可能发生变化。YouTube 发言人表示：“这些投诉中的指控根本不属实。”Snapchat 发言人表示：“我们从根本上不同意这些指控 - 我们不针对学校。”Meta 拒绝置评，TikTok 也没有回应置评请求。加利福尼亚州人民诉加利福尼亚州和科罗拉多州的 MetaAttorneys 牵头由 29 个州组成的团体在2023 年针对 Meta 和 Instagram 的诉讼。该案将于 8 月接受审判。虽然该案与加利福尼亚州 MDL 的法官是同一位法官，但各州指控 Meta 单独违反了《儿童在线隐私保护法》，这是一项名为 COPPA 的联邦法律。该法律旨在保护 13 岁以下儿童免遭在线运营企业的攻击，但于 2000 年颁布。根据法庭记录，Meta 已在此案中提供了超过 200 万份文件。如果各州的主张胜诉，则要求 Meta 更好地阻止 13 岁以下用户使用其平台，并删除之前从未成年用户那里收集的数据，以及进行一系列其他更改。Meta 使用这些数据来执行广告定位和训练其人工智能 (AI) 模型等操作。该公司发言人拒绝发表评论。 未成年人约翰·多伊诉 Roblox 等人 法新社（来自盖蒂图片社） Roblox 和 Discord 面临诉讼

contentParagraphs.en: 117.8 Million people fled their homes worldwide: Lebanon’s shelter crisis escalatesNewsFeedA new UNHCR report reveals that 117.8 million people worldwide were forcibly displaced by the end of 2025, driven from their homes by war, violence, and persecution. articles.title: The theory taking the rich by storm: China funds data center haters
articles.summary: Tech millionaires claim China is behind a wave of local opposition to U.S. data centers, while providing little direct evidence.
articles.titleZh: 席卷富人的理论：中国资助数据中心仇恨者
articles.summaryZh: 科技百万富翁声称中国是当地反对美国数据中心浪潮的幕后黑手，但几乎没有提供直接证据。

contentParagraphs.en: Protesters hold signs in front the of the Utah state Capitol on May 23 in Salt Lake City to oppose construction of the Stratos data center in Box Elder County. Tech millionaires claim China is behind a wave of local opposition to U.S. data centers, while providing little direct evidence.
contentParagraphs.zh: 5 月 23 日，抗议者在盐湖城犹他州议会大厦前举着标语，反对在博克斯埃尔德县建设 Stratos 数据中心。科技百万富翁声称中国是当地反对美国数据中心浪潮的幕后黑手，但几乎没有提供直接证据。

contentParagraphs.en: Speaking at an event titled "Harnessing American Power" last month, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum expressed dismay at what he saw as a new wave of local opposition to data centers in America.
contentParagraphs.zh: 上个月，内政部长道格·布尔古姆 (Doug Burgum) 在题为“利用美国力量”的活动中发表讲话，对他所看到的美国当地新一波反对数据中心的浪潮表示失望。

contentParagraphs.en: What's more, he claimed, not everything was as it seemed.
contentParagraphs.zh: 更重要的是，他声称，并非一切都像看上去的那样。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's not organic and local, some of this is foreign-sourced dark money," he told the crowd.
contentParagraphs.zh: “这不是有机的、本地的，其中一些是外国来源的黑钱，”他告诉人群。

contentParagraphs.en: He isn't alone in his suspicions. Over on the podcast All-In, which is hosted by a group of tech multimillionaires who call each other "besties," there was also talk of foreign influence, specifically from China.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他并不是唯一一个持怀疑态度的人。在播客 All-In 上，一群互相称呼对方为“好朋友”的科技千万富翁主持，其中也谈到了外国影响，特别是来自中国的影响。

contentParagraphs.en: "It is starting to feel or seem like there might be a CCP-funded campaign," fund manager Gavin Baker told the hosts.
contentParagraphs.zh: 基金经理加文·贝克（Gavin Baker）告诉主持人：“人们开始感觉或看起来可能有一场由中共资助的活动。”

contentParagraphs.en: The theory that China is paying local activists in America to oppose data centers is catching on like wildfire among the Silicon Valley elite, despite a lack of evidence. Wealthy investors are naming names and pointing fingers, even if they can't directly link their projects' opponents to China.
contentParagraphs.zh: 尽管缺乏证据，有关中国向美国当地活动人士支付反对数据中心的理论在硅谷精英中像野火一样流行。富有的投资者正在点名批评，即使他们无法将项目的对手与中国直接联系起来。

contentParagraphs.en: That's not to say that Chinese influence is completely absent from the U.S. conversation around AI infrastructure and data centers. On Wednesday, OpenAI said it banned a cluster of likely Chinese accounts that used ChatGPT to generate anti-data center content this past winter. The accounts were probably run by a private Chinese technology firm working for "provincial-level government clients" in China, OpenAI said. The company said the operators posed as Americans on social media and posted AI-generated comments and images highlighting energy demand and rising electricity costs.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这并不是说美国在人工智能基础设施和数据中心的讨论中完全不存在中国的影响力。周三，OpenAI 表示，去年冬天，它禁止了一系列可能使用 ChatGPT 生成反数据中心内容的中国账户。 OpenAI 表示，这些账户可能由一家为中国“省级政府客户”服务的中国私营科技公司运营。该公司表示，运营商在社交媒体上冒充美国人，并发布人工智能生成的评论和图像，强调能源需求和不断上涨的电力成本。

contentParagraphs.en: But OpenAI also said the impact of the influence campaign appeared limited and its social media posts didn't get much traction. "This was not a case of an influence operation creating a debate. The debate existed already. This was an influence operation from China trying to interfere in it. We didn't see any signs that they succeeded," said Ben Nimmo, who leads threat investigations at OpenAI, on a call with reporters.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但 OpenAI 也表示，影响力活动的影响似乎有限，其社交媒体帖子也没有引起太大关注。 OpenAI 威胁调查负责人本·尼莫 (Ben Nimmo) 在接受记者采访时表示：“这不是影响力行动引发辩论的案例。辩论已经存在。这是中国试图干预的影响力行动。我们没有看到任何他们成功的迹象。”

contentParagraphs.en: The allegations that some activists are being directly financed by China come amid a rising wave of anti-data center sentiment: a recent Gallup Poll found that 71% of Americans somewhat or strongly opposed construction of data centers in their communities.
contentParagraphs.zh: 有关一些活动人士受到中国直接资助的指控是在反数据中心情绪不断高涨之际出现的：最近的盖洛普民意调查发现，71% 的美国人在一定程度上或强烈反对在其社区建设数据中心。

contentParagraphs.en: Despite that public opposition, the theory does appear to be gaining some traction in Washington. In a letter last week, Congressman Brett Guthrie, R-Ky., the chairman of the House Committee on Energy and Commerce, called for a briefing on whether the U.S. government sees evidence for Chinese influence in the data center debate. The letter was addressed to FBI Director Kash Patel as well as David Sacks and Michael Kratsios, two wealthy tech investors who sit on President Trump's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (Sacks, who was until recently Trump's special adviser on artificial intelligence and cryptocurrency, is also a regular host of All-In).
contentParagraphs.zh: 尽管遭到公众的反对，这一理论似乎确实在华盛顿获得了一些支持。众议院能源和商业委员会主席、肯塔基州众议员布雷特·格思里 (Brett Guthrie) 在上周的一封信中呼吁就美国政府是否认为中国在数据中心辩论中发挥影响力的证据进行通报。这封信是写给联邦调查局局长卡什·帕特尔（Kash Patel）以及大卫·萨克斯（David Sacks）和迈克尔·克拉西奥斯（Michael Kratsios）的，他们是特朗普总统科学技术顾问委员会的两位富有的科技投资者（萨克斯直到最近还是特朗普的人工智能和加密货币特别顾问，也是 All-In 的常客）。

contentParagraphs.en: "Cells" in Utah
contentParagraphs.zh: 犹他州的“细胞”

contentParagraphs.en: Canadian businessman and media personality Kevin O'Leary (center) is invested in the construction of a giant data center in Utah. He has claimed two groups in the state were operating on behalf of the Chinese government against his project. The groups deny the claims, and evidence for a coordinated Chinese campaign to stop data centers in the U.S. remains thin.
contentParagraphs.zh: 加拿大商人兼媒体名人凯文·奥利里（Kevin O'Leary）（中）在犹他州投资建设巨型数据中心。他声称该州有两个团体代表中国政府反对他的项目。这些组织否认了这些说法，而且中国协调行动阻止美国数据中心的证据仍然很少。

contentParagraphs.en: BRENDAN SMIALOWSKI/AFP via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 布伦丹·斯米亚洛斯基/法新社，盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: BRENDAN SMIALOWSKI/AFP via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 布伦丹·斯米亚洛斯基/法新社，盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Many top earners promoting the foreign influence theory are either directly or indirectly invested in artificial intelligence. One prominent advocate is Canadian multimillionaire and Shark Tank star Kevin O'Leary, who is an investor in a giant data center in Utah called Stratos. It was going to be roughly twice the size of Manhattan and was being pushed through the permitting process, until local opposition began to build.
contentParagraphs.zh: 许多宣扬外国影响力理论的高收入者都直接或间接投资于人工智能。一位著名的倡导者是加拿大千万富翁和《Shark Tank》明星凯文·奥利里 (Kevin O'Leary)，他是犹他州一个名为 Stratos 的巨型数据中心的投资者。它的面积大约是曼哈顿的两倍，并且正在推进许可程序，直到当地的反对声音开始增强。

contentParagraphs.en: After a local planning council approved the project in early May, O'Leary noticed an "immediate spike in misinformation," he said during a recent Fox News interview he posted on his own social media account.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当地规划委员会于五月初批准了该项目后，奥利里注意到“错误信息立即激增”，他最近在自己的社交媒体帐户上发布了福克斯新闻的采访，他说。

contentParagraphs.en: "We dug in, I got my guys to do a deep dig into the IP addresses and here's what we found, this is fascinating," he said. "We found two cells inside of Utah."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们进行了深入研究，我让我的人员深入研究了 IP 地址，这就是我们的发现，这很有趣，”他说。 “我们在犹他州发现了两个牢房。”

contentParagraphs.en: O'Leary claimed that a nonprofit called Alliance for a Better Utah and a consultancy known as Elevate Strategies were operating on behalf of the Chinese government against the project, which his company, O'Leary Digital, is leading. He went on to name several current and former employees with both organizations.
contentParagraphs.zh: 奥利里声称，一家名为“美好犹他州联盟”的非营利组织和一家名为“Elevate Strategies”的咨询公司代表中国政府反对该项目，而该项目由他的公司奥利里数字公司牵头。他接着提到了这两个组织的几名现任和前任员工的名字。

contentParagraphs.en: Among them was Gabi Finlayson, a senior partner at Elevate Strategies, which is based in Salt Lake City and helps Democratic candidates run for office in Utah and elsewhere.
contentParagraphs.zh: 其中包括 Elevate Strategies 的高级合伙人加比·芬利森 (Gabi Finlayson)，该公司总部位于盐湖城，帮助民主党候选人竞选犹他州和其他地方的公职。

contentParagraphs.en: She told NPR she had no idea how she or Elevate ended up on O'Leary's list. "I think we have been as confused as anybody," she said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 她告诉 NPR，她不知道自己或 Elevate 为何会出现在 O'Leary 的名单上。 “我认为我们和其他人一样感到困惑，”她说。

contentParagraphs.en: Finlayson said that Elevate, like several progressive groups, had posted about the data center on its social media channels, but the firm is not playing a central role in efforts to stop construction. Moreover, its social media accounts don't generate revenue for the organization.
contentParagraphs.zh: 芬利森表示，Elevate 与几个进步团体一样，已在其社交媒体渠道上发布了有关该数据中心的信息，但该公司并未在停止建设的努力中发挥核心作用。此外，其社交媒体帐户不会为该组织带来收入。

contentParagraphs.en: "We are certainly not a Chinese cell. Nobody pays us to make any content, let alone any foreign government," she said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们当然不是中国的细胞。没有人付钱给我们制作任何内容，更不用说任何外国政府了，”她说。

contentParagraphs.en: Employees at Alliance for a Better Utah were equally perplexed.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美好犹他联盟的员工同样感到困惑。

contentParagraphs.en: "I grew up watching Shark Tank with my dad occasionally, and I was like why is this guy talking about us?" said Elizabeth Hutchings, the organization's communications director. O'Leary named people who didn't even work there anymore, and he claimed "there was evidence that millions of dollars were being funneled from the
contentParagraphs.zh: “我从小就和爸爸一起看《创智赢家》，我当时就想，为什么这家伙要谈论我们呢？”该组织的通讯总监伊丽莎白·哈钦斯说。奥利里列出了一些甚至不再在那里工作的人的名字，他声称“有证据表明，数百万美元正在从
