contentParagraphs.en: Good morning. You're reading the Up First newsletter. Subscribe here to get it delivered to your inbox, and listen to the Up First podcast for all the news you need to start your day.
contentParagraphs.zh: 早上好。您正在阅读 Up First 时事通讯。在此订阅，将其发送到您的收件箱，并收听 Up First 播客，了解开始新的一天所需的所有新闻。

contentParagraphs.en: Today's top stories
contentParagraphs.zh: 今日头条新闻

contentParagraphs.en: The United States launched another round of airstrikes on Iran this morning, after President Trump said Iran was taking "too long to negotiate." The strikes targeted several sites along Iran's coast, including military facilities in and around the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's Revolutionary Guard says it retaliated with its own fire on 18 U.S. sites in the region, naming military bases in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan. So far, there have been no reports of damage or casualties. Efforts to negotiate an end to the war remain in limbo.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在特朗普总统表示伊朗“谈判时间太长”后，美国今天早上对伊朗发动了另一轮空袭。袭击目标是伊朗沿海的多个地点，包括霍尔木兹海峡及其周围的军事设施。伊朗革命卫队表示，他们向美国在该地区的 18 个地点开火进行报复，其中包括科威特、巴林和约旦的军事基地。到目前为止，尚未收到损坏或人员伤亡的报告。通过谈判结束战争的努力仍然悬而未决。

contentParagraphs.en: Motorists ride past a giant banner depicting Iranian missiles and a sword belonging to Imam Ali, the first Imam of the Shiites, at the Vanak Square in Tehran on June 10, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 6 月 10 日，在德黑兰瓦纳克广场，驾车者驶过一面巨大的横幅，上面画着伊朗导弹和什叶派第一位伊玛目阿里的剑。

contentParagraphs.en: 🎧 It is difficult to make a definitive call on whether this escalation between the U.S. and Iran will lead to full-scale fighting, NPR's Greg Myre tells Up First. For two consecutive nights, there have been U.S. attacks on Iran, and the president says there will be more tonight if Iran doesn't agree to a deal.
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的格雷格·迈尔 (Greg Myre) 告诉 Up First，很难对美国和伊朗之间的升级是否会导致全面战斗做出明确的判断。美国连续两个晚上对伊朗发动袭击，总统表示，如果伊朗不同意达成协议，今晚将会有更多袭击。

contentParagraphs.en: Rising gasoline prices, fueled by the U.S. war with Iran, have driven inflation to its highest level in more than three years. A report released yesterday by the Labor Department showed consumer prices in May were up 4.2% from a year ago. That is the largest annual increase since April 2023.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国与伊朗的战争推动汽油价格上涨，导致通货膨胀达到三年多来的最高水平。美国劳工部昨天发布的报告显示，5月份消费者价格同比上涨4.2%。这是自 2023 年 4 月以来的最大年度增幅。

contentParagraphs.en: 🎧 Americans' wages, on average, have not kept pace with rising prices. The Labor Department says average wages have increased by only 3.4% over the last year. NPR's Scott Horsley says this is an unfortunate turn of events because, for most of the last three years, workers saw real improvement in their buying power because wages were rising faster than prices. The Federal Reserve typically addresses inflation by raising interest rates or keeping them high. But Trump has been demanding lower interest rates, which presents a challenge for new Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh as he prepares for his first rate-setting meeting next week. Horsley says he believes interest rates will likely remain elevated for an extended period.
contentParagraphs.zh: 🎧 美国人的平均工资并没有跟上物价上涨的步伐。劳工部表示，去年平均工资仅增长了 3.4%。 NPR 的斯科特·霍斯利 (Scott Horsley) 表示，这是一个不幸的事件，因为在过去三年的大部分时间里，由于工资上涨速度快于物价上涨速度，工人们的购买力得到了真正的提高。美联储通常通过提高利率或维持高利率来解决通货膨胀问题。但特朗普一直要求降低利率，这对新任美联储主席凯文·沃什（Kevin Warsh）提出了挑战，因为他正在为下周的首次利率制定会议做准备。霍斯利表示，他相信利率可能会在较长一段时间内保持在高位。

contentParagraphs.en: In just a few hours, the 2026 FIFA World Cup will kick off, drawing billions of viewers to the world's biggest sporting event. Mexico will compete against South Africa in the opening match in Mexico City. The lead-up to the tournament has been marked by intense geopolitical tensions, as well as protests in Mexico City, which could disrupt some events.
contentParagraphs.zh: 再过几个小时，2026 年 FIFA 世界杯即将拉开帷幕，吸引数十亿观众观看世界上最大的体育赛事。墨西哥队将在墨西哥城与南非队进行揭幕战。世界杯前夕的地缘政治紧张局势以及墨西哥城的抗议活动可能会扰乱一些赛事。

contentParagraphs.en: 🎧 Those with grievances against the Mexican government have taken to the streets, NPR's Eyder Peralta says. Electric company workers, sex workers and families of missing persons are making their voices heard. A major teachers' union has set up a protest camp on the outskirts of the main square, threatening to bring their demonstrations directly to Estadio Azteca, where the opening game will be held. This year's World Cup also marks the first time a host country is welcoming a team from a country with which they are at war. Iran is scheduled to play in LA next week. The Iranian national team originally planned to hold its training camp in Tucson, Ariz., but the U.S. said they weren't welcome. Iran relocated their camp to Tijuana. The U.S. also refused entry to an Iraqi team photographer, held one of their star players in immigration for hours and denied access to a Somalian referee.➡️ With a record 48 teams and 1,248 players, there's an endless number of World Cup statistics to explore. Here are a few to get you started sounding like an expert.📷 In Queens, New York, soccer is more than just a sport. As the 7 train, also known as the "International Express," runs through the borough, each stop passes through a different immigrant community, each with its own unique connection to the game. NPR's Jasmine Garsd took a Sunday journey along the 7 line to speak with local players and fans. See photos from her trip.➡️ Ahead of the matches, learn more about the origins of the World Cup and how it grew into the multibillion-dollar spectacle with this episode of Throughline.
contentParagraphs.zh: 🎧 NPR 的艾德·佩拉尔塔 (Eyder Peralta) 表示，那些对墨西哥政府心怀不满的人已经走上街头。电力公司工人、性工作者和失踪人员家属正在发出自己的声音。一个主要教师工会在主广场的郊区设立了抗议营地，威胁要将示威活动直接带到揭幕战将举行的阿兹特克体育场。今年的世界杯也标志着东道国首次迎来来自交战国的球队。伊朗队定于下周在洛杉矶比赛。伊朗国家队原本计划在亚利桑那州图森举行训练营，但美国表示不受欢迎。伊朗将营地迁至蒂华纳。美国还拒绝一名伊拉克队摄影师入境，将他们的一名明星球员在移民局关押数小时，并拒绝与索马里裁判会面。➡️ 拥有创纪录的 48 支球队和 1,248 名球员，有无数的世界杯统计数据可供探索。这里有一些可以让您听起来像专家的例子。📷在纽约皇后区，足球不仅仅是一项运动。由于 7 号列车（也称为“国际快车”）贯穿该行政区，每一站都会经过不同的移民社区，每个社区都与游戏有着独特的联系。 NPR 的 Jasmine Garsd 周日沿着 7 号线与当地球员和球迷交谈。查看她旅行中的照片。➡️ 在比赛之前，通过这一集的 Throughline 了解更多关于世界杯的起源以及世界杯如何发展成为价值数十亿美元的盛事。

contentParagraphs.en: Jenn Liv for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Jenn Liv

contentParagraphs.en: New student loan changes from last year's One Big Beautiful Bill Act will take effect on July 1. These adjustments will include the end of the short-lived Biden-era Saving on a Valuable Education (SAVE) plan, the introduction of two new Republican-created repayment plans and stricter borrowing limits for some students. Given the volume of information and the fact that not every change will affect every borrower, NPR has developed a guide to help you understand the changes that may apply to you or someone you know.
contentParagraphs.zh: 去年《美丽大法案》的新学生贷款变更将于 7 月 1 日生效。这些调整将包括结束拜登时代短暂的“有价值的教育储蓄”(SAVE) 计划、引入两项新的共和党制定的还款计划以及对一些学生更严格的借贷限制。鉴于信息量巨大，而且并非每项变化都会影响每个借款人，NPR 制定了一份指南来帮助您了解可能适用于您或您认识的人的变化。

contentParagraphs.en: 🎓 One of the biggest changes taking effect is an expansion of the traditional Pell Grant for low-income students to include short-term workforce training. This expansion helps workers gain new skills, such as becoming a certified nursing assistant or welder, through federal assistance for training programs lasting 8 to 15 weeks.🎓 The Public Service Loan Forgiveness program still exists. It will forgive your remaining student debt after 10 years if you work full-time in public service, such as nursing or teaching, and make 120 qualifying monthly payments.🎓If you are enrolled in SAVE, you might have received a notice from the U.S. Department of Education about switching plans soon. Expect another message from your loan servicer, starting a roughly 90-day countdown. If you don't take action, the department will place you in one of the least flexible repayment plans.
contentParagraphs.zh: 🎓 正在生效的最大变化之一是扩大了针对低收入学生的传统佩尔助学金，将短期劳动力培训纳入其中。这一扩展有助于工人获得新技能，例如通过持续 8 至 15 周的联邦培训计划援助，成为一名经过认证的护理助理或焊工。🎓 公共服务贷款减免计划仍然存在。如果您全职从事公共服务工作，例如护理或教学，并每月支付 120 笔合格付款，它将在 10 年后免除您剩余的学生债务。🎓如果您注册了 SAVE，您可能很快就会收到美国教育部关于转换计划的通知。等待您的贷款服务商再次发送消息，开始大约 90 天的倒计时。如果您不采取行动，该部门会将您置于最不灵活的还款计划之一。

articles.title: ICE denies having a protester database. But a letter to Congress sheds more light
articles.summary: In a previously unpublicized letter to Congress, the newly-departed head of ICE said the agency collects data on people suspected of potentially unlawful activity, which could include protesters.
articles.titleZh: ICE 否认拥有抗议者数据库。但一封写给国会的信揭示了更多的情况
articles.summaryZh: 在此前未公开的致国会的信中，新离职的 ICE 负责人表示，该机构收集涉嫌潜在非法活动的人员的数据，其中可能包括抗议者。

contentParagraphs.en: Demonstrators attend an anti-ICE rally in Lewiston, Maine on January 24, 2026. Federal officials have acknowledged collecting information on some protesters, even as they deny maintaining a database tracking U.S. citizens.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 1 月 24 日，示威者参加缅因州刘易斯顿举行的反 ICE 集会。联邦官员承认收集了一些抗议者的信息，尽管他们否认维护跟踪美国公民的数据库。

contentParagraphs.en: Last January, when federal immigration agents started an immigration crackdown in Portland, Maine, pediatric occupational therapist Xenia Pantos was driving using their spouse's car to work when they saw masked federal agents and vehicles with tinted windows parked in the road. Worried about immigrant community members, Pantos stopped for a few minutes to observe.
contentParagraphs.zh: 去年一月，当联邦移民特工在缅因州波特兰开始镇压移民时，儿科职业治疗师 Xenia Pantos 正开着配偶的车去上班，当时他们看到路边停着蒙面的联邦特工和带有有色窗户的车辆。由于担心移民社区成员，潘托斯停下来观察了几分钟。

contentParagraphs.en: Pantos told NPR they stayed at least 10 feet away from the agents and did not interact with them, but noticed an agent taking photos of another observer's license plate.
contentParagraphs.zh: 潘托斯告诉 NPR，他们与特工保持了至少 10 英尺的距离，并且没有与他们互动，但注意到一名特工拍摄了另一名观察员的车牌照片。

contentParagraphs.en: Hours later, Pantos' spouse, Carly Williams, a nonprofit consultant, said she received a call from a blocked number. A deep male voice on the other end of the line asked for her by name and identified himself as calling from the Department of Homeland Security.
contentParagraphs.zh: 几个小时后，潘托斯的妻子卡莉·威廉姆斯（Carly Williams）是一名非营利组织顾问，她说她接到了一个被屏蔽的电话。电话那头传来低沉的男声，询问她的名字，并自称是国土安全部打来的。

contentParagraphs.en: Williams said the caller asked if anyone else drives her vehicle. When Williams mentioned her spouse sometimes did, the caller asked Williams if she knew her spouse had stopped at an incident that morning.
contentParagraphs.zh: 威廉姆斯说，打电话的人询问是否还有其他人驾驶她的车。当威廉姆斯提到她的配偶有时会停下来时，打电话的人问威廉姆斯她是否知道她的配偶那天早上在发生事件时停下来。

contentParagraphs.en: "What he basically said was, 'You should let her know to not do that anymore because people who are doing that type of thing are getting added to a domestic terrorist watch list,'" Williams recalled in an interview with NPR. (While the caller referred to Pantos as "she" and "her," Pantos uses they/them pronouns).
contentParagraphs.zh: “他基本上说的是，‘你应该让她知道不要再这样做了，因为做这类事情的人会被添加到国内恐怖分子观察名单中，’”威廉姆斯在接受美国国家公共广播电台采访时回忆道。 （虽然呼叫者称 Pantos 为“她”和“她的”，但 Pantos 使用“they/them”代词）。

contentParagraphs.en: "That was a pretty terrifying phone call to receive, as you can imagine," Williams said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “你可以想象，接到这个电话是非常可怕的，”威廉姆斯说。

contentParagraphs.en: DHS declined to comment on the couple's account when asked by NPR.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当 NPR 询问时，国土安全部拒绝对这对夫妇的账户发表评论。

contentParagraphs.en: For months, Department of Homeland Security officials have repeatedly denied having a database tracking U.S. citizen protesters or a database of "domestic terrorists", even as anecdotes like what happened to Pantos and Williams suggest federal agents are collecting observers' information in some capacity.
contentParagraphs.zh: 几个月来，国土安全部官员一再否认拥有追踪美国公民抗议者的数据库或“国内恐怖分子”的数据库，尽管潘托斯和威廉姆斯等轶事表明联邦特工正在以某种身份收集观察员的信息。

contentParagraphs.en: In a previously unpublicized letter sent to members of Congress in April, recently departed acting ICE director Todd Lyons acknowledged the agency gives itself wide latitude to collect information on individuals suspected of potential violations of law, including interference with ICE operations or officer safety matters, and maintains records on people who were never arrested.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在四月份发给国会议员的一封此前未公开的信中，最近离职的 ICE 代理主任托德·莱昂斯 (Todd Lyons) 承认，该机构拥有广泛的自由度来收集涉嫌潜在违法行为的个人信息，包括干扰 ICE 行动或官员安全事务，并保留从未被捕者的记录。

contentParagraphs.en: In the letter, Lyons denied that ICE maintains a database of protesters or that DHS maintains a "separate, standalone database" of individuals who were encountered but not arrested or detained. But he said at protests that involved alleged criminal conduct, ICE has collected "information to identify individuals reasonably believed to be involved in, or directly supporting, potential violations of federal law and to address officer safety and facility security concerns." The letter said ICE collects "essential biographic and biometric information and situational details."
contentParagraphs.zh: 里昂斯在信中否认 ICE 维护抗议者数据库，也否认国土安全部维护遇到但未被逮捕或拘留的个人的“单独、独立的数据库”。但他表示，在涉及涉嫌犯罪行为的抗议活动中，ICE 收集了“信息，以识别有理由相信参与或直接支持潜在违反联邦法律的个人，并解决官员安全和设施安全问题。”信中称，ICE 收集“基本的传记和生物识别信息以及情况详细信息”。

contentParagraphs.en: Lyons wrote: "If individuals who interact with ICE officers are not arrested or detained, any information collected during those encounters is maintained consistent with applicable law and DHS and ICE policies and is treated as an official government record."
contentParagraphs.zh: 莱昂斯写道：“如果与 ICE 官员互动的个人没有被逮捕或拘留，那么在这些接触期间收集的任何信息都将符合适用的法律、国土安全部和 ICE 政策，并被视为官方政府记录。”

contentParagraphs.en: NPR is the first news organization to review the letter, which is dated April 21.
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 是第一个审阅这封日期为 4 月 21 日的信件的新闻机构。

contentParagraphs.en: It was sent in response to Rep. Maxwell Frost (D-Fla.) and 11 other Democratic members of Congress who wrote to DHS in February asking questions about what data the department collects on protesters.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该邮件是为了回应佛罗里达州民主党众议员马克斯韦尔·弗罗斯特 (Maxwell Frost) 和其他 11 名国会民主党议员于 2 月份写信给国土安全部，询问该部门收集了哪些抗议者数据的问题。

contentParagraphs.en: Civil liberties experts told NPR Lyons' letter appears to be the clearest official acknowledgement yet by federal immigration officials that they may be routinely collecting and preserving information on protesters and observers who are not arrested.
contentParagraphs.zh: 公民自由专家告诉美国国家公共广播电台，里昂斯的信似乎是联邦移民官员迄今为止最明确的官方承认，他们可能会例行收集和保存有关未被捕的抗议者和观察员的信息。

contentParagraphs.en: "This letter is evidence of the fact that ICE is knowingly collecting and maintaining official government records on any protestor or lawful observer that its agents claim is potentially interfering with them or threatening agent safety," said JoAnna Suriani, a lawyer at the nonprofit legal and advocacy organization, Protect Democracy.
contentParagraphs.zh: 非营利性法律和倡导组织“保护民主”的律师乔安娜·苏里亚尼 (JoAnna Suriani) 表示：“这封信证明，ICE 正在故意收集和保存任何抗议者或合法观察员的官方政府记录，其特工声称这些记录可能会干扰他们或威胁特工的安全。”

contentParagraphs.en: Suriani is representing Pantos, Williams and other observers in Maine in a federal lawsuit that alleges their First Amendment rights were violated by federal agents who tried to intimidate them by recording their faces and license plates and threatening to add them to a domestic terrorism database.
contentParagraphs.zh: 苏里亚尼在缅因州代表潘托斯、威廉姆斯和其他观察员提起联邦诉讼，指控联邦特工侵犯了他们的第一修正案权利，联邦特工试图通过记录他们的面部和车牌来恐吓他们，并威胁将他们添加到国内恐怖主义数据库中。

contentParagraphs.en: "Anyone who has seen the videos of our clients' interactions with ICE agents can see they aren't impeding anything and pose no threat to anyone, so why was their information collected?" Suriani said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “任何看过我们客户与 ICE 特工互动视频的人都可以看到，他们没有妨碍任何事情，也不对任何人构成威胁，那么为什么要收集他们的信息呢？”苏里亚尼说道。

contentParagraphs.en: Protesters photographed, filmed and threatened with charges
contentParagraphs.zh: 抗议者被拍照、录像并受到指控威胁

contentParagraphs.en: Since the Trump administration's immigration crackdown began last year, peaceful protesters and observers recording federal immigration operations on their cell phones have been threatened with criminal charges for impeding or interfering with law enforcement operations. However, many cases where charges were brought against activists have been dismissed or resulted in acquittals. DHS officials have also previously asserted that recording federal agents and posting the videos amounts to "doxxing" and is a threat to their safety.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自去年特朗普政府开始镇压移民以来，和平抗议者和用手机记录联邦移民行动的观察员就因阻碍或干扰执法行动而受到刑事指控的威胁。然而，许多针对活动人士的指控已被驳回或被无罪释放。国土安全部官员此前还声称，录制联邦特工并发布视频相当于“人肉搜索”，对他们的安全构成威胁。

contentParagraphs.en: Observers in several states, including Minnesota and Tennessee, complained that agents photographed their faces and license plates and later determined their identities and where they lived. Federal agents have access to a suite of surveillance tools, including facial recognition technology, and can access vehicle registration records using a car's license plate.
contentParagraphs.zh: 包括明尼苏达州和田纳西州在内的几个州的观察员抱怨说，特工拍摄了他们的脸部和车牌，然后确定了他们的身份和居住地。联邦特工可以使用一套监控工具，包括面部识别技术，并可以使用汽车牌照访问车辆登记记录。

contentParagraphs.en: An activist stands outside across from what appears to be an U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement SUV in Portland, Maine on January 23, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 1 月 23 日，缅因州波特兰，一名活动人士站在一辆看似美国移民和海关执法部门的 SUV 对面。

contentParagraphs.en: A number of observers have also said their Global Entry status
contentParagraphs.zh: 许多观察家也表示他们的全球入境状态

articles.title: Inflation tops 4% for the first time in 3 years on spike in gasoline prices
articles.summary: Inflation has surged to its highest level in more than three years since the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, triggering a surge in gasoline prices.
articles.titleZh: 汽油价格飙升导致通胀三年来首次突破 4%
articles.summaryZh: 自美国和以色列对伊朗发动战争以来，通货膨胀已飙升至三年多来的最高水平，引发汽油价格飙升。

contentParagraphs.en: Inflation tops 4% for the first time in 3 years on spike in gasoline prices
contentParagraphs.zh: 汽油价格飙升导致通胀三年来首次突破 4%

contentParagraphs.en: <iframe src="https://www.npr.org/player/embed/nx-s1-5853077/nx-s1-9805102" width="100%" height="290" frameborder="0" scrolling="no" title="NPR embedded audio player">
contentParagraphs.zh: <iframe src =“https://www.npr.org/player/embed/nx-s1-5853077/nx-s1-9805102”width =“100％”height =“290”frameborder =“0”scrolling =“no”title =“NPR嵌入式音频播放器”>

contentParagraphs.en: Energy costs accounted for more than 60% of the monthly rise in the consumer price index in May.
contentParagraphs.zh: 能源成本占5月份消费者价格指数月度涨幅的60%以上。

contentParagraphs.en: Stay up to date with our Up First newsletter, sent every weekday morning.
contentParagraphs.zh: 请随时关注我们每个工作日早上发送的 Up First 时事通讯。

contentParagraphs.en: Soaring gasoline prices, triggered by the U.S. war with Iran, have pushed inflation to its highest level in more than three years.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国与伊朗的战争引发的汽油价格飙升，将通货膨胀推至三年多来的最高水平。

contentParagraphs.en: A report from the Labor Department on Wednesday showed consumer prices in May were up 4.2% from a year ago. That's the biggest annual increase since April of 2023. By contrast, the Labor Department says average wages have risen only 3.4% over the last year, so workers' real spending power has declined.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国劳工部周三发布的报告显示，5 月份消费者价格同比上涨 4.2%。这是自 2023 年 4 月以来的最大年度增幅。相比之下，劳工部表示，去年平均工资仅上涨 3.4%，因此工人的实际消费能力有所下降。

contentParagraphs.en: Prices rose 0.5% between April and May, with higher energy costs accounting for more than 60% of that monthly increase. Gas prices have jumped by well over a dollar a gallon since the war began, strangling shipping traffic in the Strait of Hormuz — a critical pathway for much of the world's oil supply.
contentParagraphs.zh: 4 月至 5 月期间，价格上涨 0.5%，其中能源成本上涨占月度涨幅的 60% 以上。自战争开始以来，天然气价格每加仑上涨超过一美元，扼杀了霍尔木兹海峡的航运交通，而霍尔木兹海峡是世界大部分石油供应的重要通道。

contentParagraphs.en: Higher fuel costs also pushed up airfares in May. Airline tickets cost about 27% more than they did a year ago.
contentParagraphs.zh: 燃油成本上涨也推高了五月份的机票价格。机票价格比一年前上涨约 27%。

contentParagraphs.en: Grocery prices showed little change during the month, rising just 0.1%. Stripping out volatile food and energy prices, "core inflation" was 2.9% for the 12 months ending in May, a slightly larger annual increase than the previous month.
contentParagraphs.zh: 本月食品杂货价格变化不大，仅上涨 0.1%。剔除波动较大的食品和能源价格，截至 5 月份的 12 个月“核心通胀”为 2.9%，年度增幅略高于上月。

contentParagraphs.en: Stubborn inflation makes it less likely the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates anytime soon, especially since the U.S. job market appears to be stabilizing. Employers added 172,000 jobs last month.
contentParagraphs.zh: 顽固的通胀使得美联储不太可能很快降息，特别是考虑到美国就业市场似乎正在企稳。上个月雇主增加了 172,000 个工作岗位。

contentParagraphs.en: Gasoline prices have eased in recent days, amid hopes of a possible negotiated settlement between the U.S. and Iran. But with an average price of $4.15 a gallon nationwide, pump prices are still about $1.17 higher than they were before the war began, according to AAA.
contentParagraphs.zh: 由于美国和伊朗之间可能通过谈判达成和解，汽油价格最近几天有所回落。但根据 AAA 的数据，全国平均价格为每加仑 4.15 美元，汽油价格仍比战争开始前高出约 1.17 美元。

articles.title: US and Iran exchange strikes across Middle East for second day in a row
articles.summary: Iran says it responded to US strikes by targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain, which reported damage to its capital city overnight.

articles.title: Chinese activist in UK told by X that abusive deepfakes do not breach rules
articles.summary:
Apple Peiqing Ni targeted by account portraying her as promiscuous drug addict after posting about Tiananmen Square
A high-profile Chinese activist in the UK who was inundated with deepfake posts on X portraying her as a sexually promiscuous drug addict was told that the abuse di
articles.titleZh: 英国的中国活动人士接受 X 采访时表示，滥用深度伪造品并不违反规则
articles.summaryZh:
苹果公司倪培清在发布有关天安门广场的帖子后，被描述为滥交吸毒者的账户所针对
一名在英国备受瞩目的中国活动人士在 X 上被大量深度造假帖子描绘成性乱的吸毒者，她被告知，这种虐待行为

contentParagraphs.en: A high-profile Chinese activist in the UK who was inundated with deepfake posts on X portraying her as a sexually promiscuous drug addict was told that the abuse did not breach the rules of Elon Musk’s platform.Apple Peiqing Ni, the 27-year-old founder of the UK-based China Dissent Network, had been advised by UK police to complain to the US-headquartered platform after she was targeted by what she believes is a pro-regime bot.The abuse included 12 posts tagging Ni and containing fake photographs and videos of her. The captions variously described her as having “chronically chaotic sexual relationships” and being a heavy drug user.One post also incorrectly said – and celebrated – that Ni had been “beaten badly on the red streets of London while protesting with other anti-China groups”. It went on: “This humiliating defeat is the perfect retribution for her extreme behaviour.”That claim appeared to be a reference to an attack by masked men on a male activist last March at a sit-in on Trafalgar Square organised by Ni.The posts on X followed Ni posting that she would be joining a commemoration of the Tiananmen massacre on 4 June in Sutton, south London.In response to Ni’s complaints, X’s automated systems said the posts did not breach the platform’s rules on harassment or violent speech. A follow-up complaint to the platform’s support service was also rejected.This was despite X’s rules prohibiting “the malicious, unreciprocated targeting (such as mentioning or tagging) of individual(s), particularly when shared to humiliate or degrade someone”.The account was only suspended hours after the Guardian raised the decisions on the complaints with X’s press office and sought an explanation. Ni was subsequently informed that X had acted in response to “different reports” regarding the content.The saga raises questions over X’s internal systems. Ni, who moved to the UK in 2019 to study, said she could not understand why X had not immediately acted to protect her from the abuse.She said: “I posted a poster for the Tiananmen Square massacre commemoration in Sutton and right after that, that account made deepfake images about me. They’ve been tagging my username since they started it.“I called the police; they did visit me, but they said because X is an American company and they cannot identify the individual behind the account, they cannot do anything about it. They advised me to report, which I did. And I asked many of my friends and members to do it.”Ni recently set up the China Dissent Network, an organisation that aims to create a safe space for Chinese dissidents. She is also working on a free speech campaign for Hong Kong and Chinese students on UK campuses.Ni claimed that her parents had been contacted by the secret police in China over her activism in the UK. “My parents are being harassed in China, saying their business will be influenced, and my grandpa’s grave will be dug out if I continue my activism overseas,” she said. “They’ve visited my parents few times.”Ni said that one of the posts accusing her of animal abuse had correctly written that she had a cat. She said: “I feel like they’re doing some kind of profiling of me … Every time I go out, I feel like I might be followed or watched. And I actually think that might be the case.”Concerns about antisemitic and anti-Muslim material on X led to an announcement last month of an agreement between the UK regulator Ofcom and the platform over “illegal hate and terror content”.X agreed to review and assess UK suspected illegal terrorist and hate content on average within 24 hours of it being reported, but Ni said there appeared to be a lack of interest in cracking down on harassment faced by Chinese dissident voices.X did not respond to a request for comment.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一位在英国备受瞩目的中国活动人士在 X 上被大量将她描述为性乱的吸毒者的 Deepfake 帖子淹没，但她被告知，这种虐待行为并没有违反埃隆·马斯克平台的规则。27 岁的英国中国异议网络 (China Dissent Network) 创始人 Apple 裴庆妮 (Apple Peiqing Ni) 在遭到她认为是亲政权机器人的攻击后，英国警方建议她向总部位于美国的平台投诉。虐待行为包括 12 起贴上倪的标签并包含她的虚假照片和视频的帖子。文字说明以不同方式描述她“长期处于混乱的性关系”，并且是一名重度吸毒者。一篇帖子还错误地称——并庆祝——倪“在与其他反华团体抗议时在伦敦的红色街道上遭到殴打”。帖子还写道：“这次耻辱性的失败是对她极端行为的完美报应。”这一说法似乎指的是去年 3 月在倪组织的特拉法加广场静坐活动中，蒙面男子对一名男性活动人士的袭击。X 上的帖子是在倪发布她将于 6 月 4 日在伦敦南部萨顿参加天安门大屠杀纪念活动之后发布的。针对倪的投诉，X 的自动化系统表示这些帖子没有违反该平台的规则。关于骚扰或暴力言论。对该平台支持服务的后续投诉也被拒绝。尽管 X 的规则禁止“恶意、非回报地针对个人（例如提及或标记），特别是在分享以羞辱或贬低某人时”。在《卫报》向 X 新闻办公室提出投诉决定并寻求解释后几小时，该帐户才被暂停。倪随后获悉，X 是针对有关内容的“不同报道”采取的行动。这一事件引发了人们对 X 内部系统的质疑。 2019年移居英国学习的倪说，她不明白为什么X没有立即采取行动保护她免受虐待。她说：“我在萨顿张贴了一张纪念天安门广场大屠杀的海报，之后，那个账户就制作了关于我的深度伪造图片。他们从开始就一直在标记我的用户名。“我报了警；他们确实拜访了我，但他们说，因为 X 是一家美国公司，他们无法识别该帐户背后的个人，所以他们对此无能为力。他们建议我举报，我就这么做了。我要求我的很多朋友和成员这样做。”倪最近成立了中国异见网络，该组织旨在为中国异见人士创造一个安全的空间。她还在英国校园为香港和中国学生开展言论自由运动。倪声称，她的父母因她在英国的活动而被中国秘密警察联系。“我的父母在中国受到骚扰，说他们的生意会受到影响，我爷爷的坟墓会被挖掉。” “如果我继续在海外维权，”她说，“他们已经拜访过我的父母几次了。”倪说，其中一篇指控她虐待动物的帖子正确地写道，她养了一只猫。她说：“我觉得他们正在对我进行某种侧写……每次我出去，我都觉得我可能会被跟踪或监视。”我认为情况可能确实如此。”对 X 上反犹太和反穆斯林内容的担忧导致英国监管机构 Ofcom 上个月宣布与该平台就“非法仇恨和恐怖内容”达成协议。X 同意平均在报告后 24 小时内审查和评估英国涉嫌非法恐怖分子和仇恨内容，但倪表示，似乎对打击中国持不同政见者声音面临的骚扰缺乏兴趣。X 没有回应请求发表评论。

articles.title: Trump says 'I love the inflation' as US prices rise at fastest rate in three years
articles.summary: The US president later said he had meant that he actually loved that inflation was not higher.
articles.titleZh: 特朗普表示“我喜欢通货膨胀”，因为美国物价以三年来最快的速度上涨
articles.summaryZh: 这位美国总统后来表示，他的意思是，他实际上很高兴通货膨胀率没有更高。
